Title: “Climate Change” Omitted from NSF Funding Applications
Summary: The National Science Foundation (NSF) funded 40% fewer grants containing the words “climate change” in 2017, compared to 2016, with scientists apparently engaging in “self-censorship” and using alternative terms (e.g., “extreme weather”).

Title: “Climate Change” References Removed from EPA Documents
Summary: EPA staff amended internal program documents to replace “climate change” with “climate resiliency” in an effort to prevent program cuts.

Title: “Climate Change” References Removed from FHWA Website
Summary: References to “climate change” on the FHWA’s website and were replaced with alternate terms such as “resiliency” and “sustainability.”

Title: “Climate Change” References Removed from USGS Press Release
Summary: DOI amended the press release for a U.S. Geological Survey paper on sea level rise to remove a sentence identifying climate change as its cause.

Title: “Climate Change” References Removed from White House Website
Summary: The White House website was re-launched with all references to “climate change” removed.

Title: “MAHA” Report Contains Numerous Errors, Cites Nonexistent Studies
Summary: The Department of Health and Human Services has released the "Make America Healthy Again" (MAHA) Commission’s report on childhood chronic diseases. The report has been found to contain references to studies that do not exist, misrepresentations of scientific conclusions, and other errors.

Title: “Make Carbon Dioxide Great Again” Bill Introduced in Wyoming State Legislature
Summary: A Wyoming state senator introduced a bill titled “Make carbon dioxide great again–no net zero,” which includes legislative findings that the “more carbon dioxide [is] available . . ., the better life can flourish.”

Title: $27.4 Billion Cut in Funding for Energy and Environment Programs Proposed by White House
Summary: President Trump sought Congressional approval to cut $27.4 billion from several federal environmental programs, including clean energy, efficiency and research programs, grant programs, climate research, and conservation efforts.

Title: 10 Year Research Project Cancelled by DOE
Summary: DOE cancelled an ongoing project researching how tropical forests will respond to climate change. The study, which involved over 130 scientists, was launched in 2015 and due to continue until 2025, but will shut down 7 years early (in 2018).

Title: 63 NIH Vaccine Research Center Scientists Fired
Summary: Between January 20 and and April 28, 2025 the Vaccine Research Center at the National Institutes of Health lost 63 of its roughly 200 contract staff after the Department of Health and Human Services and the “Department of Government Efficiency” ordered a 35% cut to contract spending.

Title: A Member of the Texas Oil and Gas Regulatory Commission Made Several False Statements Regarding the Climate Impacts of the Fossil Fuel Industry
Summary: A member of the Texas Railroad Commission made several false or misleading statements about the industry’s impact on the environment and climate change, claiming incorrectly that human activity contributes an insignificant amount of carbon dioxide to the atmosphere.

Title: Access to BLM Conservation Website Blocked During Government Shutdown
Summary: Citing the government shutdown, the Bureau of Land Management blocked access to a website containing links to documents discussing proposed changes to the conservation rules for greater sage grouse habitat. The block appears to violate DOI guidance.

Title: Access to NOAA Climate Change Website Blocked
Summary: Due to the partial government shutdown, the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration blocked access to its National Climatic Data Center website, which includes multiple climate data sets.

Title: Accuracy of National Climate Assessment Questioned by Trump Administration
Summary: A White House spokesperson questioned the accuracy of the Fourth National Climate Assessment, claiming that it overstates the risks associated with climate change. That claim was disputed by climate scientists.

Title: Accuracy of Scientific Models Questioned by House Members
Summary: Four members of Congress requested that the House Committee on Oversight and Reform investigate the accuracy of models used to predict the impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic.

Title: Accuracy of Scientific Study Questioned by President Trump
Summary: President Trump criticized a study which suggests that hydroxychloroquine is not effective in treating COVID-19. The President claimed, without evidence, that the study was "false" because it was conducted by scientists who are "obviously not friends of the administration."

Title: Age of Earth Misrepresented by Wisconsin State Representative
Summary: During a hearing of the Wisconsin legislature, state Representative Jesse Kremer stated that it is “fact” that the earth is only 6,000 years old, contradicting decades of scientific research showing that the earth is 4.5 billion years old.

Title: All 37 NSF Divisions Abolished, Directors and Staff Reassigned
Summary: On May 8, 2025, National Science Foundation staff were informed that the agency’s 37 divisions will be abolished and the number of programs significantly reduced.

Title: All Lab Scientists at CDC Division of Viral Hepatitis Fired
Summary: On April 1, 2025, all 27 of the lab scientists at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention's Division of Viral Hepatitis were fired.

Title: Animal Welfare Documents Removed from USDA Website
Summary: Thousands of documents detailing animal welfare violations were removed from USDA’s website after being made publicly available for decades.

Title: Anti-Science Views Endorsed by President Trump
Summary: President Trump posted a tweet which appeared to endorse erroneous claims, made by Patrick Moore, that climate change is “[f]ake [s]cience” and that increasing carbon dioxide emissions will benefit society.

Title: Anti-Vaccine Theorist to Work on HHS Study of Vaccines and Autism
Summary: Health and Human Services (HHS) Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. has tapped David Geier, a prominent figure in the anti-vaccine movement, to work on a new study examining the long-debunked theory that vaccines cause autism.

Title: Arctic Strategy Removed from White House Website
Summary: The White House deleted links to the U.S. National Strategy for the Arctic and related documents from its website.

Title: Arizona Senate Republicans Pass Bill to Prevent Climate Action
Summary: Arizona Senate Republicans have passed a bill that will ban the use of public funds for any government or institutional action intended to address climate change.

Title: Availability of Scientific Data on Waterways Misrepresented by EPA Official
Summary: EPA claimed that no data was available showing the number of streams that would be affected by EPA’s proposed rule but internal EPA documents show that agency staff had conducted a detailed analysis and determined that at least 18% of streams and 51% of wetlands nationwide would be affected.

Title: Benefits of Renewable Energy Downplayed by DOE
Summary: DOE changed its website to remove references to renewable energy as a low-emission alternative to fossil fuels and focus solely on renewable energy’s potential to create jobs.

Title: Bill Allowing Teachers to Question Proven Scientific Theories Introduced in North Dakota
Summary: A bill was introduced in North Dakota that would, if enacted, prevent teachers being prohibited from discussing “the strengths and weaknesses” of any scientific theory, which could undermine science education.

Title: Bill Allowing Teachers to Question Proven Scientific Theories Introduced in South Dakota
Summary: A bill was introduced in South Dakota that would, if enacted, prevent teachers being prohibited from discussing “the strengths and weaknesses” of any scientific theory, which could undermine science education.

Title: Bill Allowing Teachers to Question Proven Scientific Theories Re-Introduced in South Dakota
Summary: A bill was introduced in South Dakota that would, if enacted, prevent teachers being prohibited from discussing “the strengths and weaknesses” of any scientific theory. According to science education groups, this could undermine science education.

Title: Bill Appointing Industry Representatives to Oversee Environmental Decisions Introduced in Michigan
Summary: A package of bills was introduced in Michigan that would, if enacted, establish a new committee to oversee the state Department of Environmental Quality. The committee would consist of over half industry representatives and have broad authority to veto rules proposed by the Department.

Title: Bill Empowering Science Denial in Classroom Introduced in Oklahoma
Summary: A bill has been introduced in the Oklahoma State Senate that would allow teachers and students to question proven scientific theories.

Title: Bill Encouraging Discussion of Creationism in Science Class Introduced in Florida
Summary: A bill (House Bill 303) was introduced in the Florida legislature that would, if enacted, allow the discussion of religious beliefs in schools. Critics allege that the bill is intended to undermine the teaching of evolution and encourage discussion of creationism in schools.

Title: Bill Labelling Proven Scientific Theories ‘Controversial’ Introduced in Texas
Summary: A bill was introduced in the Texas legislature that describes “climate change, biological evolution, the chemical origins of life, and human closing” as “controversial” theories. The bill would, if enacted, give teachers greater scope to question these and other proven scientific theories.

Title: Bill Misrepresenting Causes of Climate Change Introduced in Montana
Summary: A bill stating that “nature, not human activity, causes climate change” was introduced in Montana. If passed, that statement would be declared an official “finding” of the Montana legislature and would need to be offered “when providing educational and information materials on climate change.”

Title: Bill Misrepresenting the Science of Fetal Development Introduced in Alabama
Summary: A bill was passed by the Alabama legislature that includes an inaccurate description of fetal development. The bill states that “a baby’s heart starts to beat at around six weeks,” but a six-week-old embryo does not yet have developed organs, such as a heart.

Title: Bill Obstructing Medical Research Introduced in Iowa
Summary: A bill was introduced in the Iowa legislature that would, if enacted, prohibit the transfer or use of fetal body parts. Opponents of the bill say it will prevent vital medical research using fetal tissue.

Title: Bill Opposing Next Generation Science Standards Introduced in Connecticut
Summary: A bill was introduced in Connecticut that would, if enacted, prevent implementation of the Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS). The bill’s sponsor has previously asserted that climate change is a “controversial” subject that should not be taught in Connecticut schools.

Title: Bill Opposing Next Generation Science Standards Introduced in Iowa
Summary: A bill (House File 61) was introduced in the Iowa state legislature that would, if enacted, prevent implementation of the Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS). The bill’s sponsor, Rep. Skyler Wheeler, has previously said she “oppose[s] NGSS as it pushes climate change . . . [and] evolution.”

Title: Bill Opposing the Next Generation Science Standards Introduced in Iowa
Summary: A bill (House File 2317) was introduced in the Iowa legislature that would, if enacted, prevent implementation of the Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS). The bill was reportedly prompted by a concern that the NGSS discuss “controversial topics,” such as climate change and evolution.

Title: Bill Preventing Use of the Next Generation Science Standards Introduced in Iowa
Summary: A bill was introduced in Iowa that would prevent use of the Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS) in schools. The bill’s sponsor opposes the NGSS because they present “controversial topics [such as] climate change, man’s negative impact on the environment, and evolution as a scientific fact.”

Title: Bill Prohibiting Adoption of the Next Generation Science Standards Introduced in Iowa
Summary: A bill was introduced in Iowa that would, if enacted, prevent implementation of the Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS). The bill was reportedly prompted by a concern that the NGSS discuss “controversial topics,” such as climate change and evolution.

Title: Bill Requiring Balanced Teaching of Scientific Theories Introduced in Florida
Summary: A bill was introduced in Florida that would, if enacted, require “controversial” scientific theories to be taught in a “balanced” way. The bill does not identify specific “controversial” theories but is thought to be targeting climate change and evolution.

Title: Bill Requiring Teachers to Question Proven Scientific Theories Introduced in Iowa
Summary: A bill was introduced in Iowa that would, if enacted, require teachers to present “opposing points of view or belief” when discussing evolution, the origins of life, and global warming. The bill contains no requirement that those opposing points of view be scientifically valid.

Title: Bill Requiring Use of “Balanced” Teaching Materials Introduced in Florida
Summary: A bill was introduced in Florida that would, if enacted, require instructional materials in public schools to provide “objective, balances, and non-inflammatory viewpoints on controversial issues,” likely including anthropogenic climate change.

Title: Bill Restricting Climate Education Introduced in Connecticut
Summary: A bill was introduced in Connecticut that would, if enacted, require school students to be “exposed to the debate” regarding the causes of climate change, which may force teachers to present anthropogenic climate change as a disputed scientific theory.

Title: Bill Restricting Teaching of Climate Change Introduced in Connecticut
Summary: A bill was introduced in Connecticut that would, if enacted, eliminate climate change from the state’s science education standards. In justifying the bill, its sponsor indicated that climate change is a “controversial” issue, and thus should not be taught in Connecticut schools.

Title: Bill to Abolish Agricultural Advisory Boards Introduced in Nevada
Summary: A bill was introduced in the Nevada House of Representatives that would, if enacted, abolish the Garlic and Onion Growers Advisory Board, the Alfalfa Seed Advisory Board, and the Advisory Council for Organic Agricultural Products.

Title: Bill to Close State Environmental Laboratory Introduced in Oklahoma
Summary: A bill was introduced in the Oklahoma Legislature that would, if enacted, require closure of the State Environmental Laboratory. The laboratory is currently responsible for testing the state’s 1,700 public water supply systems.

Title: Bill to Eliminate State Environmental Quality Board Introduced in Minnesota
Summary: Bills were introduced in the Minnesota House of Representatives and Senate that would, if enacted, eliminate the state Environmental Quality Board which is responsible for coordinating work on environmental issues among state agencies.

Title: Bill Undermining Science Education Introduced in Florida
Summary: A bill was introduced in Florida that would, if enacted, require “controversial” scientific theories to be taught in a “balanced” manner. The bill does not identify specific “controversial” theories but its sponsor indicated that it would apply to climate change and evolution.

Title: Bill Undermining Science Education Introduced in Oklahoma
Summary: Oklahoma State Senate Bill 613 would allow teachers to encourage students to question proven scientific theories.

Title: Bill Undermining Science Education Introduced in Oklahoma
Summary: Oklahoma State Senate Bill 662 would allow teachers to encourage students to question proven scientific theories.

Title: Bill Undermining Science Education Introduced in Oklahoma
Summary: A bill (Senate Bill 14) was introduced in Oklahoma that would, if enacted, allow teachers to present “the scientific strengths and scientific weaknesses of existing scientific theories.” Critics say this could undermine science education.

Title: Bill Undermining Science Education Introduced in Oklahoma
Summary: A bill (Senate Bill 393) was introduced in Oklahoma that would, if enacted, prevent teachers being prohibited from discussing “the strengths and weaknesses” of any scientific theory. Critics say this will undermine science education.

Title: Bill Undermining Science Education Introduced in South Dakota
Summary: A bill (Senate Bill 55) was introduced in the South Dakota legislature that would, if enacted, prevent teachers being prohibited from discussing “the strengths and weaknesses” of any scientific theory. Critics say this will undermine science education.

Title: Bill Undermining Science Education Introduced in Texas
Summary: A bill was introduced in Texas that would, if enacted, allow the State Board of Education to reject any instructional materials it deems unsuitable. According to critics, this would allow the board to reject materials that discuss controversial scientific theories, e.g. climate change and evolution.

Title: Bill Undermining Teaching of Climate Change Introduced in Arizona
Summary: A bill (HB 2002) was introduced in Arizona that would, if enacted, require teachers to “present both sides” of any “controversial issue.” The term “controversial issue” is defined broadly to include any issue addressed in an “electoral party platform,” and thus would include climate change.

Title: Bill Undermining Teaching of Climate Change Introduced in Iowa
Summary: A bill was introduced in Iowa that would, if enacted, require teachers to "provide students with materials supporting all sides" of any "controversial issue" discussed. The term "controversial issue" is defined broadly to mean "any issue that is addressed in an electoral party platform."

Title: Bill Undermining Teaching of Climate Change Introduced in Maine
Summary: A bill was introduced in Maine that would, if enacted, require teachers to “present both sides” of any “controversial issue.” The term “controversial issue” is defined broadly to include any issue addressed in an “electoral party platform,” and thus would include climate change.

Title: Bill Undermining Teaching of Climate Change Introduced in South Dakota
Summary: A bill was introduced in South Dakota that would, if adopted, request local school boards to adopt rules preventing teachers “[a]dvocat[ing] for any issue that is part of a political party platform.” Critics fear that this could limit teachers’ ability to discuss climate change.

Title: Bill Undermining Teaching of Evolution Introduced in Alabama
Summary: A bill (House Bill 258) was introduced in the Alabama legislature that would, if enacted, allow teachers to present the theory of creation as an alternative to evolution.

Title: Bill Undermining Teaching of Evolution Introduced in Arkansas
Summary: A bill (House Bill 2050) was introduced in the Arkansas legislature that would, if enacted, allow the unproven theories of intelligent design and creationism to be taught alongside evolution in schools.

Title: Bill Undermining Teaching of Evolution Introduced in Indiana
Summary: A bill was introduced in Indiana that would, if enacted, allow the governing body of a school to require teachers to present “various theories concerning the origin of life” to students. Critics say this is likely to result in the theory of creation being presented as an alternative to evolution.

Title: Bill Undermining Teaching of Evolution Introduced in South Carolina
Summary: A bill (House Bill 3826) was introduced in the South Carolina legislature that would, if enacted, allow public schools to include creation science as part of the curriculum in elective religion courses.

Title: Causes of Climate Change Misrepresented by Montana State Legislator
Summary: During debate on a bill before the Montana House of Representatives Natural Resources Committee, Representative Joe Read acknowledged that “[t]he climate is changing,” but erroneously claimed that carbon dioxide “is not the culprit,”

Title: CDC Climate Change Summit Cancelled
Summary: CDC preemptively cancelled a planned summit on climate change and public health due to concerns about the Trump administration’s reaction.

Title: CDC Climate Office Reorganized and Staff Reassigned
Summary: The CDC’s Climate and Health Program office was merged with another office. The CDC previously issued a dismissal notice to the head of the Climate and Health Program but later withdrew it after he threatened to bring legal proceedings. The CDC subsequently reassigned him to another unit.

Title: CDC Coronavirus Guidance Ignored by White House Officials
Summary: President Trump pushed schools to re-open despite warnings from the CDC that doing so presents the "highest risk" for the spread of COVID-19. President Trump rejected CDC recommended measures to mitigate such risks, labelling them as "impractical" and "expensive.”

Title: CDC COVID-19 Database Retired
Summary: The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) directed hospitals to stop submitting COVID-19 patient information to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention's COVID-19 reporting system and instead submit it to a new HHS database. Experts are concerned that the change will impact data integrity.

Title: CDC Directed to Revise Coronavirus Guidelines
Summary: Vice President Pence stated that the CDC would revise its guidelines on re-opening schools during the COVID-19 pandemic so that they are not "too tough." The statements followed a tweet from President Trump that he "disgree[s]" with the guidelines.

Title: CDC Director's Recommendation to Extend Ban on Cruises Rejected by White House
Summary: Centers for Disease Control & Prevention (CDC) recommended extending a ban on passenger cruises that was in place to control the spread of COVID-19. Vice President Mike Pence overruled the extension, dismissing CDC’s concerns that allowing cruise ship travel would result in the spread of COVID-19.

Title: CDC Employees Prevented from Speaking to Reporters
Summary: Employees at the Centers for Disease Control were instructed not to speak with any member of the news media, regardless of the nature of their inquiry, without the approval of the communications office.

Title: CDC Global Health Initiative Scaled-Back
Summary: Officials at the Centers for Disease Control informed staff that, due to inadequate funding, the global health security initiative would be "downsized." China was among the countries cut from the initiative, which public health experts claim may have delayed U.S. response to the COVID-19 pandemic.

Title: CDC Halts Release of Expert Measles Assessment
Summary: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention ordered staff not to release an expert assessment showing an increased risk of contracting measles in areas with lower vaccination rates and emphasizing the importance of vaccinations.

Title: CDC Orders Retraction or Pause in Publication of Research Manuscripts
Summary: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) ordered agency scientists to retract or pause the publication of research manuscripts, including internal publications and any medical or scientific journal, that mention “forbidden terms” relating to gender and sexuality.

Title: CDC Pressured by White House to Change COVID-19 Testing Guidelines
Summary: White House officials reportedly pressured the Centers for Disease Control to revise its guidelines on when people should be tested for COVID-19. The revised guidelines state that testing may not be required for those who come in contact with infected individuals if they are not showing symptoms.

Title: CDC Pressured to Align Weekly COVID-19 Reports with Trump Administration's Pandemic Messaging
Summary: The Department of Health and Human Services requested that the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention submit its weekly COVID-19 reports for review prior to publication, reportedly over concerns that they would "undermine President Donald Trump's optimistic messages about the outbreak."

Title: CDC Public Health Position Eliminated
Summary: The Centers for Disease Control eliminated a position, based in China, which was intended to assist local epidemiologists track and investigate disease outbreaks.

Title: CDC Removes Data Sets, Whole Pages from Its Website
Summary: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) removed references to gender identity and equity, including studies, guidelines, and vaccine recommendations from its website.

Title: CDC Staff Given List of Forbidden Words
Summary: Policy analysts at the Centers for Disease Control were given a list of 7 words, including “evidence-based” and “science-based,” which should not be used in documents prepared for next year’s budget.

Title: CDC Staff Prevented from Working with WHO Staff
Summary: Scientists at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) were told that they can no longer co-author publications with World Health Organization (WHO) staff.

Title: Change to Science Education Standards Proposed in Ohio
Summary: A member of the Ohio Board of Education proposed adding a statement to the state’s new science education standards which emphasizes that “scientific conclusions are necessarily based upon philosophical assumptions,” which is an argument often used to challenge the teaching of evolution.

Title: Charters of BLM Advisory Councils Revised
Summary: DOI revised the charters of 21 resource advisory councils. Under the revised charters, councils are required to “provide recommendations for implementation” of various policies, leading to concerns that they will be put “in a position of implementing an agenda rather than providing solid advice.”

Title: Chemical Rules Adopted by EPA Against Scientific Advice
Summary: EPA rewrote certain chemical rules against the advice of agency scientists who warned that the changes may result in an “underestimation of the [chemicals’] potential risks to human health and the environment”

Title: Children’s Health Research Program Cancelled by EPA
Summary: EPA announced that it will no longer participate in the Children’s Environmental Health and Disease Prevention Research Program. Established in 1998, the program funds 13 research centers, which “explore ways to reduce children’s health risks from environmental factors.”

Title: Climate Change Described as “Hoax” by Louisiana Attorney-General
Summary: During a speech at the Heartland Institute’s America First Energy Conference, Louisiana Attorney General Jeff Landry referenced the “climate change hoax,” and expressed concern that people believe it.

Title: Climate Change Documents Removed from NPS Website
Summary: Documents describing the climate action plans of 92 national parks were removed from the National Park Service website.

Title: Climate Change Dropped from National Security Strategy
Summary: President Trump unveiled a new National Security Strategy, which omitted “climate change” from a list of global threats.

Title: Climate Change Information Buried on EPA Website
Summary: EPA buried a webpage intended to educate school children about climate change, removing a link to the page from an archived version of its website.

Title: Climate Change Information Removed from DOI Website
Summary: DOI’s main climate change webpage was revised to omit various references to the adverse effects of climate change and a detailed discussion of DOI’s climate change priorities.

Title: Climate Change Information Removed from USDA Website
Summary: Information on climate change mitigation and adaptation was removed, and then returned, to USDA’s “Climate Hubs” website.

Title: Climate Change Materials Removed from DOT Website
Summary: The Department of Transportation (DOT) removed at least two pages discussing climate change from its website. One page had previously served as DOT’s “Transportation and Climate Change Clearinghouse.” The second page included links to studies on the impacts of climate change on transportation.

Title: Climate Change Omitted from FEMA Report
Summary: The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) did not discuss climate change in its 2019 National Preparedness Report. Previous versions of the annual report, including those published during the Trump administration, discussed resources available to prepare for the impacts of climate change.

Title: Climate Change Omitted from List of EPA Research Objectives
Summary: EPA published a draft “Air and Energy Strategic Research Action Plan” identifying research needed to address its “strategic objectives and mandates to improve air quality.” Unlike previous versions adopted, the Action Plan does not list climate change preparation as a “research objective.”

Title: Climate Change Omitted from List of White House R&D Priorities
Summary: The White House Office of Management and Budget and Office of Science and Technology Policy released a memorandum outlining the Trump administration’s research and development priorities. Unlike previous versions, the memorandum does not list climate change as a priority area.

Title: Climate Change Omitted from National Biodefense Strategy
Summary: The White House published a new National Biodefense Strategy, outlining measures to prevent and mitigate disease outbreaks. Despite numerous scientific studies finding that climate change will increase the risk of such outbreaks, it is not discussed anywhere in the strategy.

Title: Climate Change Omitted from Oceans Research Plan
Summary: The White House published a new report identifying research priorities for oceans science and technology. The report omits a section discussing research priorities with respect to “the ocean’s role in climate,” which had appeared in all previous versions of the report.

Title: Climate Change Page Removed from BLM Website
Summary: A page describing efforts to address climate change, and providing links to DOI and other federal resources on climate change and renewable energy, was removed from the Bureau of Land Management's website.

Title: Climate Change Pages Removed from State Department Website
Summary: The State Department removed various pages mentioning climate change from its website, including a page providing links to the department’s 2010, 2014, and 2016 Climate Action Reports.

Title: Climate Change Panel Disbanded by Alaska Governor
Summary: Alaska Governor Mike Dunleavy disbanded the Climate Change for Alaska Leadership Team. The team was established in 2017 by then Governor Bill Walker to advise on strategies for addressing climate change.

Title: Climate Change Report Removed from Alaska DEC Website
Summary: One day after new Alaska Governor Mike Dunleavy took office, the state Department of Environmental Conservation deleted a key climate change report from its website. The report included a detailed plan for addressing climate change impacts in Alaska.

Title: Climate Change Resources Removed from DOE Website
Summary: Links to various climate change resources, including the National Climate Assessment, were removed from the DOE’s main climate change website.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Alabama Attorney General
Summary: Alabama Attorney General Steve Marshall filed an amicus brief in Exxon Mobil v. Schneiderman. The brief questions the scientific consensus on climate change and the conclusion that it will be “sufficiently dangerous to require urgent policy responses.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Alaska Congressman
Summary: During a televised debate, Rep. Don Young asserted, contrary to decades of scientific research, that climate change is not caused by human activities. Rep. Young also accused scientists of overstating the risks posed by climate change, saying “[i]t’s being used as an instrument to frighten people.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Arizona Attorney General
Summary: Arizona Attorney General Mark Brnovich filed an amicus brief in Exxon Mobil v. Schneiderman. The brief questions the scientific consensus on climate change and the conclusion that it will be “sufficiently dangerous to require urgent policy responses.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Arkansas Attorney General
Summary: Arkansas Attorney General Leslie Rutledge filed an amicus brief in Exxon Mobil v. Schneiderman. The brief questions the scientific consensus on climate change and the conclusion that it will be “sufficiently dangerous to require urgent policy responses.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Colorado State Senator
Summary: Colorado State Senator Vicki Marble questioned scientific research indicating that greenhouse gas emissions cause climate change. Senator Marble argued that climate change is more likely caused by solar flares. She also suggested that cats cause more health problems than greenhouse gas emissions.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Connecticut State Representatives
Summary: The Connecticut House of Representatives passed a bill requiring the teaching of climate change in public schools. During debate on the bill, several representatives questioned the scientific consensus on climate change, and humans contribution to it.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by EPA Administrator
Summary: In an interview on a Nevada television station, EPA Administrator misrepresented climate research indicating that rising temperatures will have widespread negative impacts, suggesting that they could actually be positive.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by EPA Administrator
Summary: In an interview on CNBC’s Squawk Box, EPA Administrator Scott Pruitt misrepresented climate science, suggesting that scientists have not established that greenhouse gas emissions are the primary cause of climate change.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Florida Governor
Summary: During a press conference following Hurricane Irma, Florida Governor Rick Scott suggested that climate change may be caused by natural cycles, and not anthropogenic greenhouse gas emissions.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by House Science Committee Members
Summary: During a hearing, several members of the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology misrepresented climate science. Among other things, they incorrectly suggested that human activities are not the primary driver of climate change, and that climate change is not causing sea level rise.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Kentucky Governor
Summary: Kentucky Governor Matt Bevin posted a tweet suggesting that the cold weather in winter 2017/18 disproves the existence of climate change. The tweet included a weather map showing frigid temperatures in the U.S. and said: “Trump has been in office 1 year and has already fixed global warming.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Kentucky Mayor
Summary: Todd Pollock, the mayor of Bedford in Kentucky, told reporters that the science of climate change is not “settled” and that its causes have yet to be determined.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Louisiana Attorney General
Summary: Louisiana Attorney General Jeff Landry filed an amicus brief in Exxon Mobil v. Schneiderman. The brief questions the scientific consensus on climate change and the conclusion that it will be “sufficiently dangerous to require urgent policy responses.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Maine Governor
Summary: During a speech to the Waterville Rotary Club, Maine Governor Paul LePage erroneously stated that the Earth is cooling. He also claimed that, while the Earth may have experienced warming in the past, it was not due to human activities.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Michigan Attorney General
Summary: Michigan Attorney General Bill Schuette filed an amicus brief in Exxon Mobil v. Schneiderman. The brief questions the scientific consensus on climate change and the conclusion that it will be “sufficiently dangerous to require urgent policy responses.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Nebraska Attorney General
Summary: Nebraska Attorney General Doug Peterson filed an amicus brief in Exxon Mobil v. Schneiderman. The brief questions the scientific consensus on climate change and the assumption that it will be “sufficiently dangerous to require urgent policy responses.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by New Hampshire Governor
Summary: During an interview on public radio, New Hampshire Governor Chris Sununu questioned the scientific consensus on climate change, suggesting that scientists disagree on its causes, and that further research is needed to determine the role played by greenhouse gas emissions.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by New Mexico Energy Secretary
Summary: During his confirmation hearing, the Secretary of the New Mexico Energy, Minerals, and Natural Resources Department suggested that climate change is a natural phenomenon and, contrary to numerous scientific studies, natural gas extraction has not contributed to a “methane hotspot” over the state.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by North Dakota Mayor
Summary: During a radio interview, Howard Klug, the Mayor of Williston, North Dakota, questioned whether climate change is real and asserted that human activities have little impact on the climate. He argued that there is no need to restrict fossil fuel development as the “world . . . can heal itself."

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Oklahoma Attorney General
Summary: Oklahoma Attorney General Mike Hunter filed an amicus brief in Exxon Mobil v. Schneiderman. The brief questions the scientific consensus on climate change and the conclusion that it will be “sufficiently dangerous to require urgent policy responses.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Oregon State Senator
Summary: In an official newsletter, State Senator Alan Olsen claimed that climate change is caused solely by natural forces, not by human activities. Sen. Olsen also erroneously asserted that temperatures have not increased in recent years and accused scientists of overstating the risks of climate change.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Pennsylvania State Senator
Summary: During a speech at a natural gas conference, Pennsylvania State Senator Scott Wagner erroneously asserted that climate change is not caused by greenhouse gas emissions, but rather the earth moving closer to the sun, or perhaps the “heat coming off” human bodies.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by President Trump
Summary: In a televised interview, President Trump questioned the scientific consensus on climate change, asserting that weather patterns have “changed both ways.” The President also contracted scientific research showing that climate change is contributing to more frequent and severe extreme weather events.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by President Trump
Summary: President Trump posted a tweet suggesting that recent cold weather in the U.S. disproves the existence of climate change. The President’s tweet was widely criticized by climate scientists, who noted that it confused short-term weather trends with longer-term changes in climate.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by President Trump
Summary: In a televised interview, President Trump questioned scientific research showing that anthropogenic greenhouse gas emissions are the primary driver of climate change, saying we “don’t know” what would have happened “with or without man.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by President Trump
Summary: During an international television appearance, aired on ITV, President Trump misrepresented scientific data regarding climate change. The President made several incorrect statements, including suggesting that global temperatures are not increasing, and sea ice cover is not declining.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by South Carolina Attorney General
Summary: South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson filed an amicus brief in Exxon Mobil v. Schneiderman. The brief questions the scientific consensus on climate change and the conclusion that it will be “sufficiently dangerous to require urgent policy responses.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Texas Attorney General
Summary: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton filed an amicus brief in Exxon Mobil v. Schneiderman. The brief questions the scientific consensus on climate change and the conclusion that it will be “sufficiently dangerous to require urgent policy responses.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Texas Railroad Commissioner
Summary: Texas Railroad Commissioner Wayne Christian published an op-ed arguing that “the science of climate change is far from settled” and that “we don’t know whether man-made greenhouse gases are impacting our climate in a harmful way.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Texas State Senator
Summary: Texas State Senator Jason Isaac asserted, contrary to decades of scientific research, that burning fossil fuels does not contribute to climate change. He worked with the Texas Natural Gas Foundation to develop new science education guidelines to “get the bias” against fossil fuels out of schools.

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Utah Attorney General
Summary: Utah Attorney General Sean Reyes filed an amicus brief in Exxon Mobil v. Schneiderman. The brief questions the scientific consensus on climate change and the conclusion that it will be “sufficiently dangerous to require urgent policy responses.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Vermont Governor
Summary: Vermont Governor Phil Scott misrepresented research indicating that climate change will have widespread negative impacts and suggested it could actually be “beneficial to Vermont.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Vice President Pence
Summary: Vice President Mike Pence questioned scientific research showing that climate change is primarily caused by greenhouse gas emissions, telling reporters that its causes “are yet to be seen.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Virginia Mayor
Summary: In a televised Town Hall, the Mayor of Tangier Island in Virginia questioned whether sea level rise is actually occurring, stating: “I’m a keen observer . . . why am I not seeing sings of it?”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Wisconsin Attorney General
Summary: Wisconsin Attorney General Brad Schimel filed an amicus brief in Exxon Mobil v. Schneiderman. The brief questions the scientific consensus on climate change and the conclusion that it will be “sufficiently dangerous to require urgent policy responses.”

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented by Wisconsin Senator
Summary: In a speech, Senator Ron Johnson said climate change was “bull----” (mouthing the full expletive). Sen. Johnson told CNN “I am not a climate change denier, but I also am not a climate change alarmist. Climate is not static. It has always changed and always will change."

Title: Climate Science Misrepresented in Congressional Hearing
Summary: At a House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology hearing, Rep. Lamar Smith criticized climatologists, claiming they “operate outside the principles of the scientific method.” Rep. Smith said that climate projections are “[a]larmist predictions [that] amount to nothing more than wild guesses."

Title: Climate Science Page Removed from EPA Website
Summary: A section on climate science was deleted from the website of EPA’s “SmartWay” program and other sections were amended to remove references to “climate change” and related terms.

Title: Climate Science Questioned by West Virginia Official
Summary: At a public meeting, the Secretary of West Virginia’s Department of Environmental Protection, Austin Caperton, said he “doesn’t trust” the science establishing that human activities are the primary cause of climate change.

Title: Coal Mining Study Paused by DOI
Summary: DOI’s Office of Surface Mining directed the National Academy of Sciences to stop work on a study of potential health effects from mountaintop removal coal mining. DOI had previously committed $1 million for the study, but is now reconsidering its funding decision.

Title: Coast Guard Academy Removes “Climate Change,” Related Terminology from Curriculum
Summary: The United States Coast Guard Academy has removed the phrase “climate change” and all related terminology from its curriculum to conform to Trump administration directives.

Title: Commerce Department Allows Critical Services to Lapse at NOAA Research Facilities
Summary: Federal scientists are dealing with disruptions to essential services like hazardous waste disposal, janitorial services, IT, and building maintenance at the Montlake Laboratory and other facilities after a change in Commerce Department policy.

Title: Connecticut Bill Attacks Climate Science in Education
Summary: A bill, HB 5378, introduced in Connecticut seeks to revise climate change curriculum to require “that students are exposed to the debate and research concerning the amount and effects of anthropomorphic [sic] carbon dioxide levels.”

Title: Connecticut Introduces a Bill to Revise Climate Change Education Standards
Summary: A bill has been proposed that would revise Connecticut’s science education standards to include the debate over climate change.

Title: Controversial Evolution Standard Supported by Texas Board of Education
Summary: The Texas State Board of Education voted preliminarily to retain language in its science education standards requiring students to consider “all sides” when discussing evolution. Critics say the requirement encourages the teaching of creationism as an alternative to the theory of evolution.

Title: Coronavirus Modeling Work Paused by Arizona Health Department
Summary: Scientists at Arizona State University and the University of Arizona, who had been working with the state Department of Health Services to model the COVID-19 outbreak, were directed to "pause all current work on projections and modeling."

Title: COVID-19 Data Misrepresented by Florida Governor
Summary: The Florida Department of Health launched a review of the state’s COVID-19 death data after Governor DeSantis erroneously suggested that the number of deaths is inflated. Data from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and other bodies suggests that Florida is actually undercounting deaths.

Title: COVID-19 Data Misrepresented by Georgia Health Department
Summary: The Georgia Department of Public Health published a misleading graph that appeared to show a consistent decline in the number of new COVID-19 cases. There was, however, no such decline. The graph presented an erroneous picture due to the manner in which the data was sorted.

Title: COVID-19 Data Withheld by Florida Officials
Summary: The Florida Department of Law Enforcement published a list of COVID-19 deaths in which key information, including case summaries with details of each person's medical and travel history, was redacted.

Title: COVID-19 Data Withheld by Michigan Health Department
Summary: The Michigan Department of Health and Human Services failed to release data on COVID-19 mortality rates in patients with underlying medical conditions.

Title: COVID-19 Data Withheld by NY Department of Health
Summary: A report issued by the New York Attorney General alleged that data published by the state Department of Health undercounted the number of nursing home residents who died from COVID-19 by as much as 50%. An aide to the New York Governor claimed the administrator purposely withheld the data.

Title: COVID-19 Data Withheld by Texas Health Department
Summary: The Texas Health and Human Services Commission denied a Public Information Act request for data showing a breakdown of the state's COVID-19 cases by age, geography, and race.

Title: COVID-19 Test Data Misrepresented by Virginia Officials
Summary: Virginia officials combined the results of viral tests and antibody tests when reporting COVID-19 testing rates. Public health experts say this "messes up" the data. The Governor’s chief of staff suggested the data was combined to make the state’s COVID tracking system appear more robust.

Title: Cuts to Funding for Scientific Research Proposed in Kentucky
Summary: Kentucky Governor Matt Bevin proposed a two-year budget that would cut state spending by 6.25%. The cuts would affect numerous scientific research program, including the Kentucky Mesonet, which would have its funding eliminated.

Title: Cuts to FY2018 Funding for National Hansen’s Disease Program Proposed
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2018 budget proposed a 23% cut in funding the National Hansen’s Disease Program. The program, which is operated by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services’ Health Resources and Services Administration, conducts research on leprosy.

Title: D.C. Health Department Stopped Reporting Daily Covid Case Counts
Summary: The D.C. Health Department had failed to report daily COVID case counts to its own website for two months, and had not provided daily case counts to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention for 16 days. Officials offered no reason for the change in reporting behavior.

Title: Data Omitted from Crime Report
Summary: The FBI’s 2016 Crime in the United States report contained almost 70% fewer data tables than the 2015 report.

Title: Database of Science-Based Programs Suspended by HHS
Summary: HHS suspended the National Registry of Evidence-based Programs and Practices which lists addiction and mental health programs that have been independently assessed and deemed scientifically sound.

Title: Department of Defense to Stop Sharing Satellite Data with Scientists
Summary: The U.S. Department of Defense announced plans to stop providing vital atmospheric and oceanic data to scientists and weather forecasters.

Title: Department of Energy Report Includes False Claims about Climate Change
Summary: The Department of Energy released a report that contains several false or misleading claims about human-caused climate change, including that threats from rising sea levels and extreme weather have been overstated.

Title: DHS’s FY18 Budget Cut by Trump Administration
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY 2018 budget proposed an 11% cut in funding for DHS programs supporting climate and other environmental research.

Title: Discredited Scientific Claims Promoted by Texas Health Department
Summary: The Texas Department of State Health Services published a booklet for women considering abortion. The booklet includes several disproven scientific claims, including suggesting that abortions cause breast cancer, which the American Cancer Society has forcefully rejected.

Title: Discredited Scientific Study Cited by Tennessee State Representative
Summary: Tennessee State Representative Diane Black relied on a discredited scientific study, which was funded by industry and did not comply with established research protocols, to push for the weakening of regulations controlling emissions from glider trucks.

Title: Discussion of Climate Change Omitted from Florida Governor’s Hurricane Conference
Summary: Governor Scott hosted the Governor’s Hurricane Conference to provide information on how to prepare for and recover from hurricanes. None of the conference sessions discussed climate change or sea level rise, despite significant scientific evidence that those phenomena increase hurricane severity.

Title: Discussion of Fossil Fuel Impacts Removed from Draft Idaho Education Standards
Summary: The Idaho House of Representatives Education Committee voted to remove references to the climate impacts of fossil fuel use from proposed science education standards.

Title: DOD Advisory Panel Disbanded
Summary: The U.S. Department of Defense announced that it would cancel the Jason research program. Established in the 1950s, the program brings together approximately 50 academic scientists to research and advise on various national security issues, including with respect to nuclear weapons systems.

Title: DOD Ends All Funding for Social Science Research
Summary: The Department of Defense (DOD) has ended all funding for social science research, including 91 ongoing studies into current and emerging threats such as climate change, disinformation, and extremism.

Title: DOD’s FY18 Budget Request Cut by Trump Administration
Summary: The Trump administration’s proposed FY2018 budget included $347 million in funding for climate and environmental research at DOD. This represents less than 1% of the amount requested by DOD and would result in cuts to several research programs.

Title: DOE Advisory Board Disbanded
Summary: The Secretary of Energy Advisory Board, which was a key adviser to past DOE secretaries, was reportedly “sunset.” DOE has no plans to reconstitute the board at this time.

Title: DOE Budget Cuts Proposed for FY20
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2020 budget proposes to cut funding for DOE by 10.8% (compared to FY2019 levels). Under the budget, DOE’s innovation arm — the Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (ARPA-E) — would be eliminated, and funding for its Office of Science reduced by 16.5%.

Title: DOE Budget Cuts Proposed for FY2021
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2021 budget proposes to cut funding for DOE by 8.1%. Under the budget, DOE’s innovation arm—the Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (ARPA-E)—would be eliminated, and funding for its Office of Science reduced by 16.6%.

Title: DOE Budget Cuts Proposed for FY2026
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2026 budget proposes to cut funding for the Department of Energy (DOE) by $20.29 billion. DOE’s Office of Science is a particular target of the cuts.

Title: DOE Grant Program Cancelled by Political Appointee
Summary: A Department of Energy’s (DOE) official cancelled a request for applications for funding to support research on integrating solar resources into the electric grid nearly two months after applications were due. During that period, DOE spent approximately $1 million reviewing applications.

Title: DOE Grant Programs Subject to Review
Summary: The Department of Energy's Chief of Staff ordered a review of grant programs to ensure that they align with the Trump administration's priorities.

Title: DOE Grantee Directed to Remove References to Paris Agreement from Report
Summary: Department of Energy (DOE) officials directed a climate scientist to reframe a paper examining how achievement of the Paris Agreement’s greenhouse gas emissions reduction targets would affect extreme weather events. Research for the paper was funded by DOE during the Obama Administration.

Title: DOE Office of International Climate and Technology Closed
Summary: DOE closed the Office of International Climate and Technology which was formed in 2011 to help the U.S. provide technical advice to other nations seeking to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

Title: DOE Officials Pressured Researchers to Change Report
Summary: National Energy Technology Laboratory researchers were pressured by DOE officials to change a report on electric system reliability during extreme weather events. The changes were purportedly intended to bolster the Trump administration’s claims that coal-fired power plants are vital to reliability.

Title: DOE Program Cuts Proposed for FY19
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2019 budget proposes to cut funding for DOE by 3.4% (compared to FY2017 levels). Funding for DOE’s innovation arm, the Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (ARPA-E), would be eliminated and funding for its Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy cut in half.

Title: DOE Removes References to Climate Change from Biological and Environmental Research Group Webpages
Summary: EDGI report shows that the DOE removed references to climate change from its BER group webpages

Title: DOE Reports Cancelled
Summary: Political officials at the Department of Energy reportedly prevented studies being published as official department reports. The studies were, instead, published in scientific journals that are not publicly-accessible.

Title: DOE Researchers Prevented from Publishing Work
Summary: The Department of Energy's Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy circulated a list of areas in which researchers could not publish unless they had first obtained political sign-off.

Title: DOE Scientists Prevented from Attending Conference
Summary: Up to 27 DOE scientists were told they could not attend the 2017 International Atomic Energy Agency conference.

Title: DOE Scientists Prevented from Traveling to Conferences
Summary: DOE officials ordered staff in the Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy to cancel all travel scheduled through the end of February 2019, citing the partial government shutdown. However, DOE is not affected by the shutdown, having been fully-funded through the 2019 fiscal year.

Title: DOE Staff Told to Avoid References to “Climate Change”
Summary: A supervisor in DOE’s international climate office instructed his staff not to use the terms “climate change,” “emissions reduction,” or “Paris Agreement.”

Title: DOE to Terminate Three Energy-Related Federal Advisory Committees
Summary: The Department of Energy plans to terminate the Biomass Research & Development Technical Advisory Committee, the Methane Hydrate Advisory Committee, and the Hydrogen & Fuel Cell Technical Advisory Committee to comply with an order from President Trump directing agencies to reduce advisory panels.

Title: DOE Website Rewritten to Downplay Impact of Fossil Fuels
Summary: The Energy Information Administration rewrote its “Energy Kids” website to downplay the environmental impacts of burning fossil fuels. Two charts showing the link between coal burning and greenhouse gas emissions were removed.

Title: DOE-Funded Research Paper Subject to Additional Review Due to Climate References
Summary: During the Obama Administration, the Department of Energy (DOE) funded a climate scientist’s research on links between climate change and extreme weather. After publication, DOE informed the scientist that the report would undergo review and that it\ may need to remove the acknowledgement of DOE funding.

Title: DOE-Funded Scientists Told Not to Reference “Climate Change”
Summary: DOE-funded researchers were asked by staff at the Pacific Northwest National Laboratory to remove references to “climate change” and “global warming” from project descriptions.

Title: DOE’s FY18 Budget Cut by Trump Administration
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2018 budget proposes a 59% cut in funding for DOE’s earth and environmental sciences program which plays a key role in federal climate change research.

Title: DOI Advisory Boards Suspended or Eliminated
Summary: DOI froze the work of more than 200 advisory boards, committees, and subcommittees to enable it to review “the charter and charge of each committee.” DOI also allowed the charter of the Advisory Committee on Climate Change and Natural Resource Science to expire.

Title: DOI Budget Cuts Proposed for FY19
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2019 budget proposes to reduce funding for DOI by 16.8% (compared to FY2017 levels). The reductions are achieved, in part, through cuts to various DOI grant programs.

Title: DOI Budget Cuts Proposed for FY20
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2020 budget proposes to reduce funding for the Department of the Interior (DOI) by 10.9% (compared to FY2019 levels). Funding for DOI’s research arm — the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS) — would decline by 14.4 percent (compared to FY2019 levels).

Title: DOI Budget Cuts Proposed for FY2021
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2021 budget proposes to reduce funding for DOI by 16% (compared to FY2020 levels). Funding for DOI’s research arm — the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS) — would decline by 23.6% (compared to FY2020 levels).

Title: DOI Official Embedded Misinformation in Scientific Reports
Summary: Indur M. Goklany, a science and technology policy analyst at DOI, had misleading language about climate change inserted into at least nine different scientific reports.

Title: DOI Removes References to Climate Change from WaterSMART Program Webpage
Summary: EDGI report shows that DOI removed all reference to climate change from its WaterSMART program web page.

Title: DOI Scientists Asked to "Gin Up" Emissions Figures
Summary: The U.S. Geological Survey (USGS) director asked scientists to "gin up" estimates of greenhouse gas emissions from the 2018 California wildfires. He also asked USGS scientists to compare wildfire emissions "against emissions for all energy in US" to "make a decent sound bite.”

Title: DOI Scientists Involuntarily Reassigned
Summary: Approximately 50 DOI employees, including some working on climate-related issues, were involuntarily reassigned to unrelated positions.

Title: DOI Scientists Prevented from Attending Conference
Summary: DOI capped the number of staff who could attend the American Geophysical Union’s annual meeting at 199 (down from 450 in previous years).

Title: DOI Scientists Work Criticized by Political Appointees
Summary: DOI officials criticized the U.S Geological Survey for describing climate change as “dramatically” reducing glaciers. Officials stated that this is a “perfect example of [scientists] going outside their wheelhouse” and “we need to watch for inflammatory [language] in their press releases.”

Title: DOI Scientists' Recommendations Removed from Decision Memos
Summary: DOI’s Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement (BSEE) director told staff to edit a series of decision memos supporting proposed changes to offshore drilling regulations. The edits included deleting language showing that the regulatory changes were opposed by BSEE engineers.

Title: DOI Secretary Accused of Breaching Scientific Integrity Policy
Summary: Two senior scientists at the U.S. Geological Survey resigned in protest over an alleged breach of the USGS’s scientific integrity policy by the Secretary of the Interior.

Title: DOI Staff Prevented from Attending Scientific Conference
Summary: DOI’s Bureau of Land Management prevented at least 14 scientists from attending the annual meeting of the Society for American Archaeology. Several of those scientists had been scheduled to speak at a symposium, which had to be cancelled due to the lack of participation by government scientists.

Title: DOI’s FY18 Budget Cut by Trump Administration
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2018 budget proposed a 19% cut in funding for climate and environmental research programs at DOI.

Title: DOL Advisory Boards Suspended
Summary: One DOL advisory board – the Council on Occupational Safety and Health – has been disbanded and four others have had their operations suspended. As of March 2018, the boards had not met in at least nine months.

Title: DOT Staff Ordered to “Identify and Eliminate” Initiatives Relating to Climate Change and DEI
Summary: The Department of Transportation (DOT) ordered staff to “identify and eliminate” initiatives related to climate change, racial and gender identity, DEI, and environmental justice, and to ignore the social cost of carbon in decision-making.

Title: Energy Secretary Questions Scientific Consensus on Climate Change
Summary: In an interview at a petrochemical plant in Western Pennsylvania, Energy Secretary Dan Brouillette erroneously asserted that “[n]o one knows” whether human activities are causing climate change.

Title: Engineering Positions in Virginia Beach’s Stormwater Division Eliminated
Summary: The Virginia Beach city council announced that it would suspend a storm water fee increase, which was intended to pay for additional staff to work on issues relating to sea level rise. As a result of the suspension, nine staff positions will be left unfilled.

Title: Environmental Health Journal Stops Accepting New Submissions Following Loss of Federal Contracts
Summary: The journal Environmental Health Perspectives (EHP) has announced that it will cease accepting new manuscripts following the loss of key federal contracts.

Title: Environmental Risks Misrepresented in EPA Chemical Assessment
Summary: EPA's Scientific Advisory Committee on Chemicals found that EPA’s Draft Risk Evaluation for a common solvent was "misleading." The evaluation stated that the solvent poses no environmental risks, based solely on analysis of its impact on discharges into water. SACC found this inadequate.

Title: EPA Accused of Falsely Claiming that No PFAS Were Detected in Pesticides
Summary: Public Employees for Environmental Responsibility (PEER) filed a complaint accusing EPA of falsely claiming that per- and poly-fluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) were not found in samples of several widely used pesticides.

Title: EPA Adaptation Staff Reassigned
Summary: EPA reassigned four staffers in the policy office, who had previously worked on climate change adaptation.

Title: EPA Advisory Board Chair Pressured to Change Congressional Testimony
Summary: The chair of EPA’s Board of Scientific Counselors was pressured to change her testimony before Congress to downplay the Trump administration’s decision not to reappoint half of the board’s members.

Title: EPA Advisory Committee on Agriculture Unofficially Suspended
Summary: As of June 2020, the EPA's Farm, Ranch, and Rural Communities Federal Advisory Committee had not met since May 2016. In the past, the committee has generally met two to three times per year.

Title: EPA Budget Cuts Proposed for FY19
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2019 budget proposes to cut EPA funding by 33.7% (compared to FY2017 levels), and eliminate a number of the agency’s research and education programs, including the Climate Change Research and Partnership Program and the Environmental Education Program.

Title: EPA Budget Cuts Proposed for FY20
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2020 budget proposes to cut EPA funding by 31.2% (compared to FY2019 levels), and eliminate a number of the agency’s research and education programs, including the Climate Change Research Program and the Environmental Education Program.

Title: EPA Budget Cuts Proposed for FY2021
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2021 budget proposes to cut EPA funding by 26.4% (compared to FY2020 levels), and eliminate a number of the agency’s research and education programs, including the Climate Change Research Program and STAR Research Grants Program.

Title: EPA Budget Cuts Proposed for FY2026
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2026 budget proposes to cut funding for the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) by $4.25 billion. The cuts target “woke climate research.”

Title: EPA Chemical Study Changed by Political Appointees
Summary: Political appointees at EPA directed staff in the pesticides office to change the toxicity assessment developed by scientists for perfluorobutanesulfonic acid. The changes were reportedly so significant that two of the scientists who worked on the study asked to have their names removed from it.

Title: EPA Climate Change Staff Reassigned
Summary: EPA reallocated staff from the climate change division and climate protection partnership division to other areas.

Title: EPA Climate Change Website Removed
Summary: EPA’s climate change website was removed and replaced with a message indicating that it is being updated to reflect the agency’s priorities under President Trump. [Reversed by Biden administration]

Title: EPA Demotes Staff Overseeing Scientific Research
Summary: The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) is demoting career employees who oversee scientific research at the agency and plans to replace them with political appointees.

Title: EPA Eliminates Office of Research and Development, Will Cut Over 3,700 Staff
Summary: The Environmental Protection Agency has announced plans to eliminate its Office of Research and Development and begin laying off thousands of employees.

Title: EPA Employees Report Misconduct, Retaliation, and Feelings of Futility in New Survey on Scientific Integrity
Summary: An internal survey of Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) scientists reveals threats to scientific integrity within the agency. Employees report intimidation and coercion to alter scientific findings, and fear of retaliation.

Title: EPA Employees Warned About Speaking Publicly
Summary: EPA staff received an email warning them that, while they are free to speak publicly, doing so presents risks.

Title: EPA Enforcement Agents Reassigned
Summary: Agents in EPA’s Criminal Investigation Division were reassigned to Administrator Pruitt’s security detail, forcing them to suspend investigation of climate crimes.

Title: EPA Environmental Laboratory Advisory Board Axed
Summary: Following a review of its advisory committees in accordance with Executive Order 13875, EPA announced that it would terminate the Environmental Laboratory Advisory Board (ELAB) because "there are existing committees and other mechanisms that can provide EPA with the guidance and work products that fall under ELAB’s charge."

Title: EPA Giving Preference to Industry-Backed Science
Summary: EPA proposed to repeal emissions standards for freight trucks based on research funded by the biggest truck manufacturer challenging the standards.

Title: EPA Grant Recipients Unable to Access Funding
Summary: Some Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) grant recipients are still unable to access funding, despite two temporary restraining orders halting the Trump administration’s January 20th funding freeze.

Title: EPA Grants Reviewed by Political Appointee
Summary: EPA announced that it will require new and certain existing grants to be approved by a political appointee, who has told staff he is looking for references to “climate change.”

Title: EPA Intern Directed Not to Tweet About Climate Change
Summary: An intern in EPA’s communications office was instructed by co-workers not to mention “climate change” or “going green” in tweets.

Title: EPA Managers Ignore Risk Calculations for New Chemicals
Summary: Public Employees for Environmental Responsibility filed a complaint, finding that EPA managers had ignored and deleted staff scientists’ risk calculations and ignored risks other than corrosivity when evaluating new chemicals under the Toxic Substances Control Act.

Title: EPA Places 144 Employees on Leave for Signing Letter Critical of the Trump Administration
Summary: EPA placed 144 employees on administrative leave after they signed onto a letter accusing the Trump administration of undermining the agency’s “mission of protecting human health and the environment” and “[i]gnoring scientific consensus to benefit polluters.”

Title: EPA Plans to Retire Its Online Archive
Summary: The Environmental Protection Agency announced plans to retire its online archive. After July 2023, news stories and press releases will remain on the agency’s website while content that is not required to be maintained by law will be deleted or officially recorded as records.

Title: EPA Removes Climate Change References from “Heat Island Effect” Webpage
Summary: EDGI report shows that EPA changed its website to downplay the impact of climate change on the heat island effect.

Title: EPA Research Center to be Eliminated
Summary: EPA’s National Center for Environmental Research, which distributes grants for research on the effects of chemical exposure, will be eliminated as part of an agency reorganization involving the merger of three research offices.

Title: EPA Research Grants Cancelled at Direction of Political Appointee
Summary: A political appointee at EPA — the Deputy Director for Public Affairs John Konkus — ordered the cancellation of at least four grants for research on climate change and related topics.

Title: EPA Reverses Position on Alaska Mine Following Meeting Between President Trump and Alaska Governor
Summary: The EPA informed staff that it would no longer oppose the Pebble Mine project in Alaska, disregarding previous EPA studies.

Title: EPA Science Advisors Excluded from Decision-Making
Summary: EPA adopted a new policy regarding "Science Advisory Board Engagement," under which decisions regarding the agency regulations to be reviewed by the Science Advisory Board will be made solely by the board chair. Such decisions were previously made jointly by all board members.

Title: EPA Science Advisory Board Unofficially Suspended
Summary: As of March 2018, EPA’s Science Advisory Board (SAB) had not met in at least six months, leading to concerns among members that EPA is trying to sideline the board. According to members, ordinarily, the SAB would have multiple interactions (including in-person meetings and teleconferences) during any six month period.

Title: EPA Science Panel Disbanded
Summary: EPA announced that it was disbanding the particulate matter review panel. The panel has historically provided advice to EPA’s Clean Air Scientific Advisory Committee on the need for changes to the National Ambient Air Quality Standards for particulate matter.

Title: EPA Scientific Advisory Board Members Dismissed
Summary: EPA dismissed at least five academics serving on its scientific review board and indicated that it may replace them with industry representatives.
Update Status: Additional Anti-Science Action|Legislative Update|Reversed (Partially or Fully)

Title: EPA Scientific Integrity Officer Acknowledged Delay of Internal Scientific Integrity Reports
Summary: Francesca Grifo, scientific integrity officer at the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), reported that the agency has yet to release its 2019 or 2020 reports on EPA scientific integrity. Grifo said she hoped reports from 2019-2021 would all be published by midfall 2021.

Title: EPA Scientist’s Participation in Public Events Restricted
Summary: An EPA scientist was warned not to speak on climate change at public-facing events or to have such events approved by headquarters in advance.

Title: EPA Scientists Overruled by Political Appointees
Summary: EPA officials ignored the advice of agency scientists who recommended that a Wisconsin county be classified as a “nonattainment” area. After lobbying by the Wisconsin governor, EPA decided to classify the area as “in attainment” with the ozone standards. The scientists indicated that there was no “sound technical basis” for the ruling.

Title: EPA Scientists Responsible for Preparing Chemical Assessments Reassigned
Summary: Staff in EPA’s Integrated Risk Information System (IRIS) program were forced to stop work on chemical risk assessments after agency officials commenced a review of the program’s priorities. 28 of IRIS’s 30 staff were subsequently directed to spend 25 to 50 percent of their time working with EPA’s Office of Pollution Prevention.

Title: EPA Scientists' Advice Disregarded
Summary: A majority of EPA’s Clean Air Scientific Advisory Committee (CASAC) rejected agency scientists’ finding that existing limits on fine particulate matter (PM) may not adequately protect public health, based on an assessment of recent studies on the health impacts of exposure to PM. CASAC members—who are not experts in air pollution epidemiology—labelled the assessment "inadequate."

Title: EPA Scientists' Advice Disregarded
Summary: EPA staff repeatedly warned White House officials that there were major flaws in a model being relied upon to justify weakening existing vehicle fuel efficiency standards. The warnings were ignored by officials, who moved forward with a proposal to weaken the standards, concluding that it was "not . . . worth pausing the process" to address the errors.

Title: EPA Scientists’ Advice Disregarded
Summary: EPA officials ignored the advice of agency scientists when developing new rules with respect to the use of asbestos. The scientists argued that all new uses of asbestos should be banned “because the extreme harm from this chemical substance outweighs any benefit.” That argument, however, rejected by officials.

Title: EPA Scientists’ Concerns About Mississippi Delta Flood Control Project Ignored by Senior Leadership
Summary: Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) senior managers made or directed changes that resulted in inaccuracies and deletions in an EPA staff letter directed to the Army Corps of Engineers. The letter provided comments on a drafted supplemental environmental impact statement (EIS) which assessed a flood control project’s compliance with the Clean Water Act.

Title: EPA Scientists’ Research Subject to Review Before Publication
Summary: The head of communications for the EPA transition team indicated that agency scientists would be required to have their research reviewed before publication.

Title: EPA Scientists’ Speaking Engagements Cancelled
Summary: EPA cancelled talks by 3 scientists who were scheduled to discuss climate change at a conference on the state of the Narragansett Bay.

Title: EPA Staff Forced to Prioritize Industry “Wish List”
Summary: EPA staff have been directed to prioritize industry requests for changes to environmental regulations. Many of the requested changes are contrary to agency scientific research.

Title: EPA Staff Participation in Scientific Integrity Survey Restricted
Summary: EPA scientists were warned against participating in a survey on scientific integrity within the federal government. An email containing a link to the survey was classified as spam “stemming from an unknown entity” by EPA’s Computer Security Incident Response Center, despite those running the survey providing advance notice to that it would be sent.

Title: EPA Staff Prevented from Attending Climate Conference
Summary: Half of the 34 EPA staffers scheduled to attend the Alaska Forum on the Environment were told they could no longer attend the event.

Title: EPA Staff Prevented from Updating Climate Change Indicators Website
Summary: EPA staff have been prevented from updating an agency website, titled “Climate Change Indicators in the United States,” which is intended to “compile a key set of indicators related to the causes and effects of climate change.” During the Obama Administration, new indicators were added to the website every six months, but no updates have been made since President Trump took office.

Title: EPA Staff Threatened with Mass Layoffs
Summary: The Trump administration has informed over 1,100 Environmental Protection Agency employees working on climate change, air pollution, and other environmental issues that they may be laid off.

Title: EPA Staffers Frozen Out of Decision-Making
Summary: EPA staffers are being frozen out of decision-making in their areas of expertise, with political appointees making decisions instead.

Title: EPA Talking Points Misrepresent Climate Science
Summary: EPA’s Office of Public Affairs issued a set of approved talking points on climate change. The talking points appear to be intended to cast doubt on the scientific consensus on climate change, with one stating “[w]hile there has been extensive research . . . clear gaps remain,” including in “our understanding of the role of human activity” in contributing to climate change.

Title: EPA Website Relaunched Without Climate Change Materials
Summary: Multiple links to materials aimed at helping local government officials adapt to climate change were removed from EPA’s website.

Title: EPA-Funded Scientists Barred From Serving on Advisory Committees
Summary: EPA announced that scientists receiving agency grants would not longer be able to serve on its advisory boards.
Update Status: Legislative Update|Additional Anti-Science Action|External Response|Litigation Update

Title: EPA’s FY18 Budget Cut by Trump Administration
