Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2018 budget proposes to cut funding for EPA climate and environmental research programs by 39% or $438 million.

Title: EPA's National Advisory Council for Environmental Policy and Technology Axed
Summary: Following a review of its advisory committees in accordance with Executive Order 13875, EPA announced that it would terminate the National Advisory Council for Environmental Policy and Technology. EPA claimed that the Council's functions could be fulfilled by other committees and groups.

Title: EPA’s Office of Children’s Health Omitted from New Organizational Chart
Summary: EPA issued a new organizational chart which omitted any reference to the Office of Children’s Health Protection (OCHP). The Director of OCHP, Dr. Ruth Etzel, was subsequently placed on administrative leave without explanation.

Title: EPA’s Office of the Science Advisor to be Closed
Summary: EPA is reportedly planning to close its Office of the Science Advisor. According to its website, the office is responsible for “science policy development and implementation” at EPA, and aims to “ensure that the highest quality science is better integrated into the Agency’s policies and decisions.”

Title: EPA’s Use of Scientific Studies Restricted
Summary: EPA Administrator Scott Pruitt announced a so-called “science transparency policy” that would prevent studies based on non-public scientific data being used in agency rulemakings.

Title: EPA’s Website Reclassifies Research Groups to Remove Climate Change Group
Summary: EDGI report shows that the EPA reclassified its research categories on its website to remove the climate change group.

Title: Existence of Climate Change Questioned by President Trump
Summary: In a tweet discussing recent cold weather in the Midwest, President Trump appeared to suggest that climate change is not occurring, stating “[w]hat the hell is going on with Global Waming? [sic] Please come back fast, we need you!” The President’s statement was widely criticized by climate scientists, who emphasized that cold weather does not disprove the existence of climate change.

Title: Existence of Climate Change Questioned by President Trump
Summary: President Trump posted a tweet suggesting that recent cold weather in the U.S. disproves the existence of climate change. The President’s tweet was widely criticized by climate scientists, who argued that it confused short-term weather trends with longer-term changes in climate.

Title: Faulty Science Used to Justify Mississippi Abortion Bill
Summary: Mississippi State Senator Joey Filingane argued that technological advances justify the adoption of stricter limits on abortion. In support this argument, the Senator referenced technology that he admitted does not yet exist, but argued that it may be developed in the next two to three years, before any litigation concerning the new abortion limits concludes.

Title: FCC to Auction Electromagnetic Spectrum Needed to Transmit Weather Data
Summary: The Federal Communications Commission announced plans to auction licenses for the use of electromagnetic spectrum in the 24 gigahertz band, currently used to transmit weather data collected by satellites, weather balloons and radars, and ocean buoys. Scientists have warned that using the band for other purposes could lead to corruption of the weather data or interfere with its transmission.

Title: FDA Approves New Alzheimer’s Drug over Agency Scientists’ Objections
Summary: The Food and Drug Administration approved a new Alzheimer’s drug over objections from agency staff and external advisors, who argued that there is insufficient evidence of the drug’s effectiveness. Several advisory committee members resigned in protest over the approval.

Title: FDA Cancels Annual Vaccine Advisory Committee Meeting
Summary: The Food and Drug Administration’s Vaccines and Related Biological Products Advisory Committee meeting, held annually to prepare for the upcoming flu season, was canceled.

Title: FDA Commissioner Pressured to Approve Coronavirus Treatment Before Clinical Trial Data Fully Analyzed
Summary: The Food and Drug Administration granted emergency authorization of convalescent plasma as a treatment for COVID-19 patients under pressure from President Trump. Public health experts have criticized the move, claiming that FDA Commissioner Stephen Hahn misrepresented the treatment's efficacy and pushed for its approval before it was fully tested.

Title: FDA Food Advisory Committee Disbanded
Summary: The food advisory committee, the only body dedicated to advising the Food and Drug Administration on food-related science policy, was disbanded.

Title: FDA Pulls Draft Guidance from Its Website Requiring Testing of Medicines and Medical Devices in Diverse Populations
Summary: Following an executive order from the Trump administration that dismantled Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) policies at federal agencies, the Food and Drug Administration withdrew draft guidance that required drug and device makers to ensure testing in diverse populations.

Title: Federal Officials Suppress Report Detailing Risk to Fish Species from Trump Administration’s California Irrigation Plan
Summary: Federal officials suppressed report detailing how Trump administration plan to divert water for agriculture in California would risk the state’s salmon runs.

Title: Federal Scientific Agencies Subject to Budget Cuts in FY2024 Appropriations Acts
Summary: Science-focused agencies across the federal government received budget cuts in Congress’s appropriations laws for fiscal year 2024.

Title: Federal Scientists and Officials Will Not Participate in UN Climate Science Meeting
Summary: The Trump administration blocked State Department officials and National Aeronautics and Space and Administration (NASA) scientists from attending a meeting of the United Nations (UN) Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) at which the outline for the next IPCC Assessment Report on climate change will be discussed.

Title: Fellows in Elite CDC Public Health Scientist Training Program Fired
Summary: At least 15 of 21 fellows in the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)’s Laboratory Leadership Service, a prestigious fellowship program to train public health scientists, were fired.

Title: FEMA Science Panel Unofficially Suspended
Summary: The Technical Mapping Advisory Council (TMAC) — a scientific panel charged with advising the Federal Emergency Management Agency — has not met in almost 5 months. During that period, TMAC has lacked a quorum, reportedly because the White House and Department of Homeland Security have delayed issuing security clearances to new members.

Title: Fewer Scientists Employed by BOEM During Trump Presidency
Summary: The Department of the Interior's Bureau of Ocean Energy Management lost 9 scientists, equivalent to 2.1 percent of its total scientific workforce, during the presidency of Donald Trump.

Title: Fewer Scientists Employed by EPA During Trump Presidency
Summary: The Environmental Protection Agency lost 672 scientists, equivalent to 5.9 percent of its total scientific workforce, during the presidency of Donald Trump.

Title: Fewer Scientists Employed by EPA in 2017
Summary: Over 200 scientists left EPA in 2017, most of whom have not been replaced. Over the course of 2017, EPA hired just 7 scientists.

Title: Fewer Scientists Employed by FWS During Trump Presidency
Summary: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service lost 231 scientists, equivalent to 3.8 percent of its total scientific workforce, during the presidency of Donald Trump.

Title: Fewer Scientists Employed by Institute of Education Sciences During Trump Presidency
Summary: The Institute of Education Sciences within the Department of Education, lost 33 scientists, equivalent to 19.2 percent of its total scientific workforce, during the presidency of Donald Trump.

Title: Fewer Scientists Employed by USGS During Trump Presidency
Summary: The U.S. Geological Survey lost 672 scientists, equivalent to 2.4 percent of its total scientific workforce, during the presidency of Donald Trump.

Title: Fewer Scientists Employed in Federal Government During Trump Presidency
Summary: During the first three years of the Trump administration, 1,600 scientists left the federal government. That represents 1.5 percent of the total federal scientific workforce.

Title: Findings of EPA Advisory Committee Misrepresented
Summary: In a letter to Senator Tom Udall, EPA misrepresented the findings of the Pesticide Program Dialogue Committee, in relation to the need to delay certain provisions of the Agricultural Worker Protection Standard.

Title: First CDC Vaccine Meeting Postponed
Summary: The first public meeting of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices was slated for the end of February but has been postponed with no new date.

Title: Fish and Wildlife Service Officials Ordered to Downgrade or Delist Endangered Species
Summary: Fish and Wildlife Service Officials were pushed to downgrade or delist many endangered species in the southeastern United States.

Title: Florida Citrus Department Budget Cuts Proposed for 2017-18
Summary: The Florida Department of Citrus’ 2017-18 budget proposed to reduce funding for scientific research. The reductions are reportedly necessary due to lower than expected revenues.

Title: Florida Citrus Department Budget Cuts Proposed for 2018-19
Summary: The Florida Department of Citrus’ 2018-19 budget proposed to reduce funding for scientific research by $48,700. The reductions are reportedly necessary due to lower than expected revenues.

Title: Florida COVID-19 Task Force Disbanded
Summary: Florida Surgeon General Dr. Scott Rivkees convened a task force of scientists to advise state officials on the COVID-19 pandemic. Following the task force's first meeting, members were told that it would be disbanded, because officials did not "want anything getting out in public."

Title: Florida Health Officials Ordered Not to Discuss COVID-19
Summary: Alberto Moscoso, communications director at the Florida State Health Department, ordered state health officials not to speak publicly about COVID-19 until after the November 3 Presidential election.

Title: Florida Legislature Votes to Remove Mentions of Climate Change from Existing Laws
Summary: The Florida legislature has passed a bill to remove mentions of climate change from most of the state’s existing laws.

Title: Florida Medical Examiners Commission Directed to Stop Reporting COVID-19 Deaths
Summary: Florida state officials directed the Medical Examiners Commission to stop publishing a list of COVID-19 deaths. The directive reportedly came after media reports showed that the death count recorded by the Medical Examiners Commission was 10 percent higher than the official figure published by the Florida Department of Health.

Title: Florida Officials Barred from Referencing “Climate Change”
Summary: Florida Governor Rick Scott banned state employees from using the terms “climate change” and “global warming” in official correspondence.

Title: Florida Scientist Dismissed After Refusing to Change COVID-19 Data
Summary: Rebekah Jones, a data scientist working at the Florida Department of Health, claims she was dismissed after refusing to "manually change" data in the state's online COVID-19 dashboard.

Title: Florida Surgeon General Altered State-Funded Study on COVID Vaccine Risk
Summary: The Florida Surgeon General, Joseph Ladapo, altered a state-funded study on the risks of the COVID vaccine, overstating the risks the vaccine represented for young men. Ladapo’s critics called these changes politically motivated.

Title: Florida Surgeon General Calls for Halt to mRNA COVID-19 Vaccines
Summary: Florida Surgeon General Joseph Ladapo called for a halt to the use of mRNA COVID-19 vaccines based on a widely debunked theory concerning their safety.

Title: Florida Surgeon General Disregards Public Health Norms Amid Measles Outbreak
Summary: In the midst of a measles outbreak, Florida Surgeon General Joseph Ladapo avoided urging parents of unvaccinated children to get them vaccinated against measles.

Title: Florida Surgeon General Manipulates Findings in Covid-19 Vaccine Safety Study
Summary: Florida’s Surgeon General Joseph Ladapo changed language in a state Covid vaccine study to falsely assert a risk of cardiac death for young men.

Title: Florida Surgeon General Misrepresented Risks of Covid Booster
Summary: Florida’s Surgeon General Joseph Ladapo warned people aged under 65 not to get the Covid-19 Booster in direct contradiction to CDC and FDA guidance.

Title: Florida’s Department of Education Approves Use of Classroom Material that Experts Say Distorts Science
Summary: Florida has approved the use of K-5 classroom material from the Prager University Foundation, which education experts say produces videos that distort science.

Title: Forensic Science Committee Disbanded
Summary: Attorney General Jeff Sessions announced he would not renew the National Commission on Forensic Science, a 30 member advisory committee established to review forensic science standards.

Title: Forest Service Scientist Prevented from Attending Conference
Summary: A U.S. Forest Service scientist was denied approval to travel to an international fire conference where he was scheduled to speak on severe fire weather conditions and climate change.

Title: Four Climate Fellowships Rescinded by NOAA
Summary: Budget cuts resulted in NOAA rescinding four of the eight offers made to researchers to participate in the 2017 Climate and Global Change Postdoctoral Fellowship Program. As a result, fewer researchers participated in the 2017 program than in any other year since it was launched.

Title: Funding for Coronavirus Research Study Withdrawn by NIH
Summary: The National Institutes of Health withdrew funding for a multi-year research project testing bats in China for new strains of coronavirus.

Title: Funding for Offshore Drilling Safety Study Suspended
Summary: DOI ordered the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine to “stop-work” on a study of offshore oil and gas drilling commissioned by BSEE pending review.

Title: Funding for Oklahoma Science Fair Eliminated
Summary: The Oklahoma Science and Engineering Fair, and several regional science fairs, are to be cancelled in 2018 due to a lack of funding from the state government.

Title: FWS Decision to Delist Gray Wolf Based on Faulty Science
Summary: A panel of five scientists published a report arguing that the US Fish and Wildlife Service’s decision to take gray wolves off the endangered species list is not supported by the science.

Title: FWS Officials Interfered in Scientific Study
Summary: Scientists from Oklahoma State University and the University of Georgia, who were engaged by the Fish and Wildlife Service to contribute to a study of the impact of farming on an endangered insect, allege that FWS officials dismissed concerns about the study’s methodology and pressured them to “work on a rushed timeline at odds with what they saw as good science.”

Title: FWS Scientists Prevented from Discussing or Researching “Politically Contentious” Topics
Summary: A survey of scientists at DOI’s Fish and Wildlife Service found that many have been told not to work on topics deemed “politically contentious” and to omit “politically contentious” words, including “climate change” and “science,” from their reports.

Title: GA Rep. Misrepresented Data on Death Rate from COVID-19 Vaccine
Summary: Georgia representative Marjorie Taylor Greene erroneously tweeted that the Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (available through the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services) showed high levels of COVID-19 vaccine-related deaths. Medical experts have widely asserted that vaccines are safe and effective, in contrast to Rep. Greene’s claims.

Title: GA Rep. Misrepresented Science on COVID-19
Summary: Georgia Representative Marjorie Taylor Greene posted two tweets stating that COVID-19 is only dangerous to individuals who are obese or over 65. Both claims have been rejected by public health experts as incorrect.

Title: GA Rep. Misrepresented Science on Efficacy of COVID Vaccines and Mask Wearing
Summary: Georgia Representative Marjorie Taylor Greene tweeted that the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) should not approve COVID-19 vaccines because they “do not reduce the spread of the virus & neither do masks.” Rep. Greene’s statements have been widely rejected by public health experts as incorrect.

Title: Head of U.S. Global Change Research Program Removed
Summary: The Director of the U.S. Global Change Program, Micheal Kuperberg, was removed from his position and directed to return to his previous post as a scientist at the Department of Energy.

Title: Health Risks Underestimated in EPA Chemical Assessment
Summary: EPA's Scientific Advisory Committee on Chemicals found that the Draft Risk Evaluation for the solvent carbon tetrachloride underestimated risks, and that EPA had ignored evidence of the solvent's toxicity.

Title: HHS Article on Scientific Integrity Blocked from Publication Pending Review by Trump Administration Officials
Summary: The authors of a journal article on scientific integrity and research standards announced that it would not be published as planned, pending a review by the Trump administration. The article was written by Health and Human Services employees during the Biden administration.

Title: HHS Fires Thousands of Employees, Including Agency Leaders
Summary: Thousands of employees at the Department of Health and Human Services began receiving termination emails. The Department has announced plans to fire a total of 10,000 employees.
Update Status: Reversed (Partially or Fully)|Litigation Update|Additional Anti-Science Action|Other

Title: HHS Halts Ebola Research
Summary: The Department of Health and Human Services ordered an indefinite pause on research conducted at a National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases facility into ebola and other infectious disease treatment and prevention.

Title: HHS Scientist Leading COVID-19 Vaccine Efforts Reassigned
Summary: The scientist heading the Department of Health and Human Services’ coronavirus vaccine efforts was reassigned after questioning the Trump administration’s push for use of chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine to treat the virus.

Title: HHS Secretary Ends Agency Funding for Climate Change and Public Health Programs
Summary: Health and Human Services (HHS) Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. terminated funding for three climate change and public health programs.

Title: HHS Secretary Kennedy Makes Misleading Statements about Covid-19 Booster Shots for Healthy Children
Summary: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. announced that the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention would no longer recommend Covid-19 booster shots for healthy children and pregnant women, erroneously claiming that the repeat booster strategy for children “lack[ed] any clinical data” to support it.

Title: HHS Secretary Kennedy Makes Misleading Statements about MMR Vaccine
Summary: Department of Health and Human Services Secretary Kennedy incorrectly stated that the MMR vaccine contains “a lot of aborted fetus debris” in the midst of a measles outbreak.

Title: HHS Secretary Makes Misleading Claims about Measles Vaccine, Touts Alternative Treatments
Summary: Health and Human Services (HHS) Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. made several false claims about measles and the measles vaccine, and promoted an alternative treatment regimen that medical experts have indicated could be toxic.

Title: Hospital Capacity Data Withheld by Texas Health Department
Summary: The Texas Department of State Health Services refused to release data on the state's hospital capacity during the COVID-19 pandemic.

Title: Human Role in Climate Change Downplayed by NOAA
Summary: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration issued a news release publicizing the Annual Greenhouse Gas Index. The release noted that the index has increased 40% since 1990 but, unlike previous releases, failed to attribute this increase to human activity.

Title: Humans’ Role in Climate Change Questioned by Kentucky Governor
Summary: In a speech at the annual meeting of the Southern States Energy Board, Kentucky Governor Matt Bevin suggested that climate change is not caused by human activities. The Governor described studies linking human activities to climate change as “nonsense.”

Title: Humans’ Role in Climate Change Questioned by Virginia Mayor
Summary: The Mayor of Tangier Island in Virginia, James Eskridge, told reporters that he does not believe human activities are the main cause of climate change.

Title: Hundreds of NOAA, NWS Employees Terminated
Summary: On February 27, 2025, hundreds of employees at the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, including weather forecasters and climate scientists, were fired.

Title: Idaho Lawmaker Introduces a Bill that Would Ban Most Covid Vaccines, Including Prohibiting mRNA Vaccines
Summary: Idaho State Senator Brandon Shippy introduced a bill that would place a ten year moratorium on “human gene therapy products.” The bill erroneously defines “gene therapy” to include vaccines, including mRNA Covid vaccines.

Title: Idaho to Weaken K-12 Science, Other Academic Standards
Summary: Idaho has passed a law that repeals the state’s former science academic standards for K-12 students and replaces them with new standards in an effort led by conservative lawmakers opposed to the standards’ references to climate change.

Title: Impact of Fossil Fuels Downplayed by DOI
Summary: DOI removed several references to the adverse effects of fossil fuels and the need to develop cleaner renewable energy technologies from its energy website.

Title: Impacts of Climate Change Downplayed by FEMA
Summary: The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) did not discuss climate change in its After Action Report on the 2017 hurricane season. Speaking about the report, FEMA’s Deputy Administrator for Resilience appeared to suggest that climate change is a natural phenomenon, stating “the climate shifts, it changes over time.”

Title: Information About Environmental Impacts of US-Mexico Border Wall Suppressed by DOI Officials
Summary: Department of the Interior officials deleted scientifically valid sections in a letter from biologists and wildlife managers at the US Fish and Wildlife Service to the U.S. Customs and Border Protection about the environmental impacts of construction at the US-Mexico border wall.

Title: Intelligent Design Bill Introduced in the Oklahoma State Senate
Summary: The Oklahoma State Senate introduced a bill that would require creationism and/or intelligent design be taught by any public or charter school teacher who also teaches evolution.

Title: Intelligent Design Bill Introduced to West Virginia Senate Education Committee
Summary: The West Virginia Senate Education Committee debated a bill that would allow for instruction on “intelligent design” in science classrooms.

Title: Interior Department Arctic Drilling Study Concludes there is not a Climate Crisis
Summary: The Bureau of Land Management released the final environmental impact statement for drilling in the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge and concluded that there is not a Climate Crisis

Title: Interior Secretary Downplays the Risks of Climate Change
Summary: Secretary of the Interior Doug Burgum told a House of Representatives committee that the U.S. has “plenty of time” to address the climate crisis. He also appeared to question whether climate change is happening, referring to “potential temperature change.”

Title: Iowa Department of Education Removes Phrases “Climate Change” and “Evolution” from Proposed New Science Education Standards
Summary: The Iowa Department of Education has removed the terms "evolution" and "climate change" almost entirely from the new science education standards proposed for Iowa public schools, and replaced them with terms like "biological change over time" and "climate trends."

Title: Iowa Governor Misrepresents Scientific Consensus on Benefits of Mask Wearing
Summary: Iowa Governor Kim Reynolds called into question the scientific consensus that mask wearing is effective in limiting the spread of COVID-19. The Governor's statements contradict published reports from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and public health experts more broadly.

Title: Kansas Bioscience Authority Privatized
Summary: The Kansas legislature approved a proposal to privatize the Kansas Bioscience Authority (KBA) as part of a plan to address shortfalls in the state budget. The KBA was established in 2004 to support research in the bioscience sector.

Title: Louisiana Enacts Bill Undermining Local Air Quality Monitoring
Summary: Louisiana Governor Jeff Landry signed the Community Air Monitoring Reliability Act (CAMRA), which prohibits air pollution data from community monitoring programs from being used in enforcement or regulatory actions related to the Clean Air Act.

Title: Louisiana Health Department Mischaracterizes the Health Benefits of Flu and Other Vaccinations
Summary: Louisiana public health officials have banned employees promoting certain vaccines. In a statement from the state Department of Health defending the policy, an official incorrectly stated that the flu vaccine cannot prevent individuals from getting the flu.

Title: Maine Introduces Bill to Prevent Public School Teachers from Engaging in "Political, Ideological or Religious Advocacy"
Summary: Maine’s Legislature introduced House Paper 395 which, if enacted, would require the state board of education to establish rules preventing public school teachers from engaging in “political, ideological and religious advocacy.” Critics say the law could undermine the teaching of climate change and evolution in public schools.

Title: Maine Introduces Bill to Prevent Public School Teachers from Engaging in What It Describes as "Political, Ideological[,] or Religious Advocacy"
Summary: Maine’s Legislature introduced House Paper 1034 which, if enacted, would mandate the state board of education to establish rules preventing public school teachers from engaging in “political, ideological and religious advocacy.”

Title: Major Flaws Identified in EPA Toxics Review Process
Summary: A report issued by the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine found that a chemical safety review process used by the Environmental Protection Agency from 2018 to 2020 unfairly promoted industry interests and lacked transparency. [Reversed by the Biden administration]

Title: Massachusetts Restricting State Scientists’ Communications
Summary: Under the current Governor of Massachusetts, state scientists are required to obtain permission from state public relations officials before speaking with journalists; however, a recent story in the Boston Globe revealed that interview requests by the media have been routinely denied.

Title: Materials Removed from HHS Website
Summary: Between June and December 2017, various content was removed from the website of HHS’s Office of Women’s Health, including pages discussing breast cancer and lesbian and bisexual health.

Title: Medical Science Misrepresented by Idaho Abortion Bill
Summary: A bill that would require doctors to tell patients that medication abortions can be reversed was passed by the Idaho legislature. Opponents point to the lack of scientific evidence and clinical studies in support of this treatment.

Title: Medical Science Misrepresented by Kentucky Abortion Bill
Summary: A bill that would require medication abortion prescriptions to include information about the possibility for the abortion to be “reversed” was passed by the Kentucky legislature. Opponents point to the lack of scientific evidence and clinical studies in support of this treatment.

Title: Medical Science Misrepresented by Nebraska Abortion Bill
Summary: A bill that would require doctors to tell patients that medication abortions can be reversed was passed by the Nebraska legislature. Opponents point to the lack of scientific evidence and clinical studies in support of this treatment.

Title: Medical Science Misrepresented by North Dakota Abortion Bill
Summary: A bill that would require doctors to tell patients that medication abortions can be reversed was passed by the North Dakota legislature. Opponents point to the lack of scientific evidence and clinical studies in support of this treatment.

Title: Medical Science Misrepresented by Oklahoma Abortion Bill
Summary: A bill that would require doctors to tell patients that medication abortions can be reversed was passed by the Oklahoma legislature. Opponents point to the lack of scientific evidence and clinical studies in support of this treatment.

Title: Medical Science Misrepresented by Utah Abortion Bill
Summary: A bill that would require doctors to tell patients that medication abortions can be reversed was passed by the Utah legislature. Opponents point to the lack of scientific evidence and clinical studies in support of this treatment.

Title: Medical Science Misrepresented in Kansas Abortion Bill
Summary: The Kansas legislature enacted a bill requiring doctors to tell patients that medication abortions can be reversed, but medical groups say this has not been scientifically proven.

Title: Medical Science Misrepresented in Ohio Abortion Bill
Summary: A bill intended to limit insurance coverage for abortion was introduced into the Ohio legislature. The bill endorses a non-existent procedure for ectopic pregnancies that has never been performed and is not medically viable.

Title: Mentions of Climate Change Removed from Federal Agencies’ Websites
Summary: The Trump administration has begun removing references to climate change and related terms from multiple federal agency websites.

Title: Milwaukee Health Department Employees Prevented from Discussing Lead Contamination
Summary: Former Milwaukee Health Commissioner Bevan Baker ordered Health Department employees not to discuss concerns about lead contamination with members of the city council.

Title: Minnesota Senate Introduces Bill Requiring Instruction on “the Creator” for Grades 9 - 12
Summary: A Minnesota State Senator introduced a bill that would require schools to instruct students in grades 9 to 12 about the “contrast between the scientific facts on how sickness, disease, pain, suffering, and death relate to the existence of complex living organisms, and how sickness, disease, pain, suffering, and death are a consequence imposed by the Creator of complex living organisms."

Title: Missouri Life Sciences Research Board to be Eliminated
Summary: Missouri Governor Eric Greitens convened a task force to explore ways to shrink the size and scope of the state government. The task force recommended, among other things, eliminating the state Life Sciences Research Board.

Title: Missouri Stream Gauges To Be Deactivated
Summary: Due to state budget cuts, the Missouri Water Science Center will be forced to deactivate 20% of the state’s river and stream gauges, beginning in July 2017.

Title: Montana Representative Introduces Bill to Ban mRNA Vaccines
Summary: Montana legislators held a hearing on House Bill 371, which includes several inaccurate claims about the safety of mRNA vaccines and seeks to ban their use on humans.

Title: Names of DOE Climate Change Staff Requested
Summary: The Trump transition team sent, then disavowed, a survey seeking information about DOE staff who had worked on climate change.

Title: Names of USDA Staff Working on Climate Change Requested
Summary: The Trump transition team requested information about USDA’s Office of Climate Change, including the names of all staff members.

Title: NASA Budget Cuts Proposed for FY19
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2019 budget proposes to reduce funding for NASA by 0.3% (compared to FY2017 levels). Among other things, the budget would eliminate funding for the international space station from 2025, cancel a project to upgrade communications satellites, and terminate five earth sciences research missions.

Title: NASA Budget Cuts Proposed for FY20
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2020 budget proposes to increase funding for NASA by 1.4 percent (compared to FY2019 levels). However, while total funding for NASA would increase, several key research programs would face cuts. In particular, the budget eliminates funding for the WFIRST space telescope, and two earth science missions.

Title: NASA Budget Cuts Proposed for FY2021
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2021 budget proposes to increase funding for NASA by 11.5% (compared to FY2020 levels). However, while total funding for NASA would increase, several key research programs would face cuts. For example, the budget eliminates all funding for NASA's Office of Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics, as well as two earth science missions.

Title: NASA Climate Research Program Cancelled
Summary: NASA cancelled its Carbon Monitoring System (CMS), a $10 million-a-year program, which provides funding for research aimed at improving the measurement of carbon stocks and fluxes.

Title: NASA Eliminates Office of the Chief Scientist, Other Departments
Summary: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) eliminated the office of its chief scientist and shut down two other departments, including one related to diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility (DEIA) and the Office of Technology, Policy, and Strategy.

Title: NASA Employees Avoiding Discussion of Climate Change
Summary: Since President Trump took office, NASA staff have avoided publishing climate change information online, and discussing the topic on social media. This is reportedly due to self-censorship by career staffers who are concerned about “provoking the [Trump] administration.”

Title: NASA Pressured to Remove Climate Change Information from Website
Summary: A member of the Trump administration’s National Security Council, William Happer, emailed the deputy administrator of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration, James Morehard, to complain about the inclusion of climate change information on its website.

Title: NASA Scientists Prevented from Attending Conference
Summary: Federal government scientists from the National Aeronautics and Space Administration and other agencies were prevented from attending the annual meeting of the American Astronomical Society due to the partial government shutdown.

Title: NASA Study to Monitor Air Pollution After Hurricane Harvey Cancelled
Summary: NASA cancelled a project to assess air pollution in the Houston area following Hurricane Harvey after officials at the Environmental Protection Agency and Texas Commission on Environmental Quality expressed concern that NASA’s project would “overlap with . . . existing efforts” to monitor pollution and would not be “useful.”

Title: NASA to Lose 2,145 Senior Staff
Summary: In July 2025, Politico reported that the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) was set to lose 2,145 employees from senior roles.

Title: NASA’s FY18 Budget Cut by Trump Administration
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2018 budget proposes to cut funding for NASA climate and environmental research programs by 8% or $151 million.

Title: National Cancer Institutes Staff Must Flag “Controversial” Materials
Summary: Staff at the National Cancer Institute have been instructed to flag all manuscripts, presentations, and other materials for further scrutiny if they address topics identified as “controversial, high profile, or sensitive.” The identified topics include vaccines, measles, and bird flu.

Title: National Climate Assessment Advisory Committee Disbanded
Summary: The Trump administration disbanded the fifteen-person Advisory Committee for the Sustained National Climate Assessment established by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration in 2015.

Title: National Climate Assessments Removed from Federal Websites
Summary: As of June 30, 2025, the National Climate Assessments were no longer accessible on federal websites.

Title: National Park Service Sent "Cease and Desist" Letter to Climate Scientist
Summary: The National Park Service sent a “cease-and-desist” letter to its principal climate change scientist, Dr. Patrick Gonzalez, after he testified before Congress about the impacts of climate change on U.S. national parks. The letter warned Dr. Gonzalez that his testimony, as well as other work he was doing for the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change, violated agency policy.

Title: National Science and Technology Council to be Restructured
Summary: The White House announced plans to restructure the interagency National Science and Technology Council, which was established in 1993 to coordinate science policy across the federal government. The restructure will be overseen by the Office of Science and Technology Policy, which has requested input from 20 federal agencies.

Title: National Science Foundation Threatened with Mass Layoffs
Summary: A high level official at the National Science Foundation has indicated that the agency is planning to lay off up to half of its staff in the coming months.

Title: Nearly $900 Million Cut from the Education Department’s Research Arm
Summary: The Trump administration cut nearly $900 million from the Institute of Education Sciences (IES), the research arm of the Education Department.

Title: Nearly All Staff Overseeing Climate.gov Terminated
Summary: On May 31, 2025, nearly all of the staff responsible for creating and maintaining the US government’s climate science education website were fired. No new content is expected to be published on the site.

Title: New Conference Approval Process Adopted by USGS
Summary: The U.S. Geological Survey, within the Department of the Interior, will require scientists wishing to attend certain conferences to provide their presentation title(s) and an explanation of how their research relates to the priorities of Interior Secretary Zinke. The information will be reviewed by a political appointee, who will determine which scientists can attend each conference.

Title: New Grants Process Adopted by DOI
Summary: DOI staff were directed to ensure that grants to outside groups promote the Trump Administration’s policies and have certain grants of $50,000 or more approved by political appointees.

Title: New Guidelines on the Use of Scientific Information Issued by OMB
Summary: The White House Office of Management and Budget issued new guidelines on the use of “influential scientific, financial, or statistical information” by federal agencies, directing agencies to make the research underlying such information publicly available. Critics fear this could prevent agencies relying on scientific studies that are based on confidential data which cannot be publicly disclosed.

Title: New Teaching Materials Law Proposed in Florida
Summary: A bill (CS/CS/HB 989) was introduced in the Florida legislature that would, if enacted, allow residents to challenge teaching materials discussing evolution, climate change, and other topics they consider inappropriate.

Title: NIAID Director Criticized by White House Officials
Summary: In an apparent attempt to discredit Dr. Anthony Fauci, a White House official expressed "concern[] about the number of times [he] has been wrong on things" and gave reporters a list of claims Dr. Fauci has made about COVID-19 which ultimately proved to be incorrect. The claims were, however, based on the best data available at the relevant time and were also made by other public health officials.

Title: NIAID Director Prevented from Speaking Publicly
Summary: White House officials reportedly prevented the Director of the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases, Dr. Anthony Fauci, from appearing on CBS News to discuss the COVID-19 pandemic. The host of CBS News' Face the Nation told viewers the network has "not been able to get our request for Dr. Fauci [to come on the show] approved by the Trump administration in the last three months."

Title: NIAID Director Prevented from Testifying Before Congress
Summary: White House officials prevented the Director of the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases, Dr. Anthony Fauci, from testifying before the House of Representatives Appropriations Committee about the Trump administration's handling of the coronavirus pandemic.

Title: NIAID Director Targeted by GA Representative
Summary: Georgia Representative Marjorie Taylor Greene demanded that Dr. Anthony Fauci be removed from his post as Director of the National Institutes of Allergy and Infectious Diseases over claims that he presented a “false narrative” surrounding the origins of COVID-19 and funded research to create the COVID-19 “bioweapon,” though she offered no factual evidence to support these claims.

Title: NIEHS Director’s Involvement in Scientific Study Investigated
Summary: The House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology announced it was reviewing the involvement of Dr. Linda Birnbaum, the director of the National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences, in a study of federal toxics regulation and requested that HHS also commence a review.

Title: NIH Budget Cuts Proposed for FY19
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2019 budget proposes to maintain funding for the National Institutes of Health (NIH) at FY2017 levels, despite imposing additional responsibilities on the NIH, including establishing three new sub-institutes to take over functions currently performed by other HHS offices.

Title: NIH Budget Cuts Proposed for FY20
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2020 budget proposes to cut funding for the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) by 11.9% (compared to FY2019 levels). Funding for the National Institutes of Health, within HHS, would decline by 12.6% (compared to FY 2019 levels).

Title: NIH Budget Cuts Proposed for FY2021
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2021 budget proposes to cut funding for HHS by 10% (compared to FY2020 levels). Funding for the National Institutes of Health, within HHS, would decline by 6.5% (compared to FY 2020 levels).

Title: NIH Could Lose Thousands of Senior Scientists
Summary: The White House directed the National Institutes of Health (NIH) not to renew thousands of its senior scientists, research fellows, and tenure-track investigators.

Title: NIH Cuts Billions in Grants for Covid-19 Research
Summary: The National Institutes of Health (NIH) and the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) cancelled billions of dollars in grant funding for research related to the Covid-19 pandemic.

Title: NIH Ordered to Cancel $2.6 Billion in Contracts
Summary: The National Institutes of Health has been ordered by President Trump to cancel $2.6 billion in contracts as part of a Department of Government Efficiency plan to reduce 35% of contracts in each division of the Department of Health and Human Services.

Title: NIH Scientists Temporarily Banned from Acquiring Human Fetal Tissue for Experiments
Summary: The Food and Drug Administration canceled a contract for the acquisition of human fetal tissue. The National Institutes of Health subsequently asked agency scientists to “pause procurements of fetal tissue.” This disrupted ongoing research projects at the National Eye Institute, the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases, and the National Cancer Institute.

Title: NIH Scientists Urged to Scrub References to mRNA Vaccines from Grant Applications
Summary: National Institutes of Health (NIH) scientists have been urged to strike all references to mRNA vaccines from grant applications out of concerns that they could be flagged or rejected.

Title: NIH Terminates Research Grants for LGBTQ+, Gender Identity, and DEI Studies
Summary: The National Institutes of Health (NIH) has terminated active research grants for studies of LGBTQ+ issues, gender identity, and DEI, and has ordered the review of all new and ongoing projects for “DEI research activities.”
Update Status: Reversed (Partially or Fully)|Litigation Update|Additional Anti-Science Action

Title: NIH Terminates Research on Health Impacts of Climate Change
Summary: The National Institutes of Health (NIH) issued guidance preventing the funding of research on the health effects of climate change.

Title: NIH’s FY18 Budget Cut by Trump Administration
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2018 budget proposed a 25% cut in funding for research programs at NIH’s National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences.

Title: NOAA Budget Cuts Proposed for FY19
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2019 budget proposes to reduce funding for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration by 20% compared to FY2017 levels. Among other things, the budget would eliminate $273 million in funding for NOAA grant programs, including the National Sea Grant College Program and National Estuarine Research Reserve System.

Title: NOAA Budget Cuts Proposed for FY20
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2020 budget proposes to cut funding for several NOAA-administered research programs. In particular, the budget proposes to eliminate funding for “Sea Grant, Coastal Zone Management Grants, and the Pacific Coastal Salmon Recovery,” to “allow NOAA to better target remaining resources to core missions and services.”

Title: NOAA Budget Cuts Proposed for FY2021
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2021 budget proposes to cut funding for DOC by 48% (compared to FY2020 levels). The cut would be achieved, in part, by eliminating several NOAA research programs, including the Sea Grants, Coastal Zone Management Grants, Education Grants, and Pacific Coastal Salmon Recovery Fund.

Title: NOAA Budget Cuts Proposed for FY2025
Summary: President Biden’s proposed FY2025 budget decreases overall funding for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) and includes considerable cuts to research and educational programs.

Title: NOAA Cancels Monthly Press Calls with Scientists
Summary: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) has canceled its monthly press calls featuring scientists discussing global climate conditions.

Title: NOAA Climate and Global Change Postdoctoral Fellowship Program Hit with Furloughs, Funding Delays
Summary: 10 fellows from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) Climate and Global Change Postdoctoral Fellowship Program have been placed on unpaid leave. No new fellowship offers have been made for the year, and funding delays have interrupted ongoing research projects.

Title: NOAA Climate Change Website Suspended During Government Shutdown
Summary: During the government shutdown, the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration temporarily suspended access to a website providing climate change data, while continuing to allow access to several other sites. The climate change website returned to service on January 23, 2018, following the end of the government shutdown.

Title: NOAA Cuts Decimate Great Lakes Environmental Research Laboratory
Summary: Cuts in funding for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration have led to a 35% reduction in staff at the Great Lakes Environmental Research Laboratory, and strict spending limits have made it difficult for staff who remain to purchase even basic equipment.

Title: NOAA Decommissions Sea Ice Datasets
Summary: The National Oceanic Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) is ending its support for certain snow and ice data products provided by the National Snow and Ice Data Center at the University of Colorado Boulder. The Center announced that, as a result of NOAA’s actions, it will reduce the level of service for affected products.

Title: NOAA Employees Ordered to Stop All Contact with Foreign Nationals
Summary: Employees at the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) were ordered to stop all contact with foreign nationals and seek prior approval for any “international engagement.”

Title: NOAA Fisheries Report Edited by Political Appointees
Summary: NOAA scientists prepared a report warning that an endangered whale species would be put at risk if air blast guns were used to locate potential oil drilling sites in the Atlantic Ocean. A ‘NOAA political team’ subsequently edited the report prior to its publication.

Title: NOAA Scientists Attacked By Louisiana Attorney General
Summary: Louisiana Attorney General Jeff Landry tweeted a link to a Breitbart article accusing NOAA scientists of lying about the extent of declines in Arctic sea ice cover. The article erroneously claims that Arctic sea ice cover is increasing and that temperatures in the region are no higher than previous levels.

Title: NOAA Scientists Prevented from Attending Scientific Conference
Summary: Scientists at the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) were told they would “not be able to participate” in the annual meeting of the American Meterological Society (AMS) due to the partial government shutdown which forced the cancellation of “all official NOAA travel to the AMS.”

Title: NOAA Staff Directed Not to Discuss COVID-19
Summary: NOAA's National Marine Fisheries Service instructed staff to "[m]ake no reference to anything COVID related," including "stay-at-home orders" and "travel restrictions," in public documents unless absolutely necessary. Even then, staff were told to only use "general terms to discuss the current situation," and avoid "direct mention of COVID-19 or pandemic."

Title: NOAA Will Stop Tracking Cost of Climate-Related Disasters
Summary: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) will no longer update the Billion Dollar Weather and Climate Disasters database, which has tracked weather-related disasters and their economic impact since 1980.

Title: NOAA's Acting Chief Scientist Reassigned
Summary: Craig McLean, the Acting Chief Scientist at the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, was reassigned after asking the agency's chief of state and other political appointees to acknowledge its scientific integrity policy.

Title: NOAA’s FY18 Budget Cut by Trump Administration
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2018 budget proposed to cut funding for NOAA’s climate and environmental research programs by 7% or $49 million.

Title: Non-Scientist Nominated for Top USDA Science Position
Summary: President Trump nominated Sam Clovis, a former radio announcer and economics professor, with no “hard-science” qualifications to the USDA’s top science position.

Title: North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality’s Budget Cut
Summary: The North Carolina legislature rejected a proposal from Governor Roy Cooper to increase funding for the state Department of Environmental Quality to enable it to hire scientists to work on water pollution issues. Instead, the legislature voted to cut the Department’s funding by $1.8 million, which critics say will lead to job losses.

Title: North Carolina Republicans Pass Bill Prohibiting Masks in Public Places
Summary: North Carolina State Senate Republicans have passed a bill that prohibits the wearing of masks in public places, with no exceptions for health and safety reasons.

Title: North Dakota Legislature Considers Bill to Include “Intelligent Design” in Science Standards
Summary: North Dakota’s Senate Bill 2355 would require that “intelligent design” be included as required content in the state’s science standards for elementary and secondary students.

Title: NPS Climate Change Tweets Deleted
Summary: Badlands National Park deleted four tweets containing basic scientific facts about climate change from its Twitter account. The tweets were reportedly posted by a former employee who was not authorized to use the park’s account.

Title: NPS Scientist Asked to Remove References to Climate Change from Paper
Summary: Dr. Patrick Gonzalez, the principal climate change scientist at the National Park Service, was directed to remove references to "anthropogenic" climate change from a report on climate change trends in national parks.

Title: NPS Scientist Excluded from Tour
Summary: DOI prevented the Superintendent of Glacier National Park, a noted climate change expert, from participating in Mark Zuckerberg’s tour of the park.

Title: NPS Scientists Avoiding Discussion of “Climate Change”
Summary: A survey of scientists at DOI’s National Park Service found that over one-quarter engage in self-censorship, avoiding discussion of, and work on, climate change even when not explicitly told to do so.

Title: NPS Scientists Prevented from Speaking about Climate Change
Summary: Dr. Marcy Rockman, a climate change adaptation coordinator at the National Park Service, was denied approval to travel to the Netherlands to present on her work on strategies for protecting cultural resources from climate change at a conference organized by the European Association of Archaeologists.

Title: NPS Staff Reprimanded for Climate Change Tweet
Summary: The Secretary of the Interior reprimanded the head of Joshua Tree National Park after climate change-related tweets were posted from the park’s account.

Title: NSC Directorate for Global Health Security and Biodefense Dissolved
Summary: The Trump administration disbanded the National Security Council Directorate for Global Health Security and Biodefense, which was established in 2014 to track global health risks and coordinate the federal government's response.

Title: NSF Advisory Committees Discontinued
Summary: 11 advisory committees to the National Science Foundation (NSF) were discontinued.

Title: NSF Budget Cuts Proposed for FY20
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2020 budget proposed to cut funding for the National Science Foundation by 9% (compared to FY2019 levels).

Title: NSF Budget Cuts Proposed for FY2021
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2021 budget proposed to cut funding for the National Science Foundation by 6.5% (compared to FY2020 levels).

Title: NSF Program Cuts Proposed for FY19
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2019 budget proposes to cut funding for DOE by 3.4% (compared to FY2017 levels). Funding for DOE’s innovation arm, the Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (ARPA-E), would be eliminated and funding for its Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy cut in half.

Title: NSF’s FY18 Budget Cut by Trump Administration
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2018 budget proposed a 10% cut funding in for the National Science Foundation (NSF)’s climate and environmental research programs. This is expected to lead to 800 fewer awards to universities and other institution in 2018 compared to 2017.

Title: Nuclear Scientists Fired from National Nuclear Security Administration
Summary: More than 150 employees have resigned or been fired from the National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA).

Title: NWS Cuts Weather Balloon Operations
Summary: The National Weather Service (NWS) announced that it will be stopping or reducing weather balloon operations at 11 locations due to staffing shortages.

Title: NY Public Health Officials Excluded from COVID-19 Planning
Summary: New York Governor Andrew Cuomo told a news conference that he does not "really trust the experts" working on COVID-19. His administration has sought to exclude state health officials from decisions regarding management of the COVID-19 pandemic and associated vaccine roll-out.

Title: NYC Health Department’s Surveillance Director Reassigned Over Monkeypox Disagreement
Summary: A doctor in the NYC Health Department’s Communicable Disease Division was reassigned after criticizing the Department’s monkeypox messaging. The doctor advised men who have sex with men to temporarily limit their number of partners to reduce transmission, guidance that is aligned with the World Health Organization’s statement and inconsistent with the city’s guidance “to cover up sores.”

Title: Offshore Wind Opponents Use Whale Deaths as Part of Disinformation Campaign
Summary: Disinformation surrounding recent whale deaths on the Atlantic shoreline is being used to push back against the construction of offshore wind turbines. Though experts say there is no connection, certain elected officials in New Jersey have asserted that offshore wind is dangerous to whales, and have signed a letter requesting a moratorium on offshore wind development.

Title: Ohio Abortion Bill Would Force Doctors to Reimplant Ectopic Pregnancies or Face Murder Charges
Summary: A new abortion bill in Ohio calls for harsh sentences for performing or having an abortion and would require doctors to reimplant ectopic pregnancies into the uterus despite the fact that this procedure does not exist in medicine.

Title: Ohio Legislature Considers Bills Requiring Universities to Remain Neutral on “Controversial” Climate Change
Summary: Two identical bills have been introduced in Ohio’s House and Senate which would, if enacted, require public universities to stay neutral on “controversial” issues, including climate change.

Title: Ohio Passes a Bill Expanding the Ability to Drill for Oil and Gas in State Parks
Summary: Ohio Governor Mike DeWine has signed a bill that redefines natural gas as “green energy” and forces state agencies to permit drilling on state lands.

Title: Ohio Senate Debates Bill that Would Force Universities to Give Weight to Climate Denialism
Summary: Ohio senate debated a bill called the Ohio Education Enhancement Act. By guaranteeing that universities “encourag[e] students to reach their own conclusions” on “controversial issues,” including climate change, this legislation would force Ohio educators to give undue weight to climate denialism.

Title: Oil and Gas Production Risks Downplayed on GAO Website
Summary: A GAO website on managing federal oil and gas resources was edited to remove content on the environmental and public health risks of shale oil and gas production.

Title: Oklahoma Health Department Staff Laid Off
Summary: Following discovery of a $30 billion budget gap, the Oklahoma Department of Health announced that it would lay off 198 staff, and would be unable to fill existing open positions, including several in the epidemiology department.

Title: Oklahoma Senate Introduces Bill that Would Threaten Climate Change Education in Public Schools
Summary: The Oklahoma Senate has introduced a bill that would divert funds from public schools that teach climate change.

Title: One-Third of Advisory Committees to be Terminated
Summary: President Trump issued an executive order directing federal agencies to terminate “at least one-third” of their current advisory committees. Critics argue that this will limit the scientific information and advice available to federal agencies and thus undermine science-based decision-making.

Title: Online Medical Guidelines Database Removed by HHS
Summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality, within the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, removed an online medical guidelines database. The database, known as the National Guideline Clearinghouse, was thought to be the world’s most comprehensive online repository of medical guidelines.

Title: Online Pollution Tracking Database Removed
Summary: The National Library of Medicine, within the National Institutes of Health, removed a publicly accessible interactive pollution-tracking map from its website.

Title: OSHA Advisory Committees Suspended
Summary: The Occupational Safety and Health Administration announced plans to reevaluate the work of its advisory committees.

Title: OSHA Removes References to Climate Change from Page About Heat-Related Illnesses
Summary: EDGI report shows that OSHA removed references to the impact of climate change from its page on heat related illnesses in the workplace

Title: Political Appointees Interfered in EPA Pesticide Review
Summary: A review by the Environmental Protection Agency’s Office of Inspector General found that agency officials diverged from standard scientific practices in a 2018 decision to re-approve the pesticide dicamba.

Title: Presentation Slides Misrepresenting the Causes of Climate Change Published by EPA
Summary: The Environmental Protection Agency published slides from a presentation titled Global Warming: What is it All About? The slides, which were presented by former Massachusetts Institute of Technology Professor Richard Lindzen, erroneously suggest that greenhouse gas emissions are not the primary cause of climate change.

Title: President Trump Falsely States that Alabama May be Under Threat from Hurricane Dorian
Summary: On three separate occasions in September 2019, President Trump stated that Alabama was under threat from Hurricane Dorian, even though no government weather forecast predicted any impact would be felt in the state. To support his claims, the President referred to a map of the hurricane's projected track, which appeared to have been altered using a sharpie marker.

Title: President Trump Nominates Policy Specialist with No Science or Engineering Degrees to Be His Science Advisor
Summary: President Trump nominated Michael Kratsios to be his science advisor. Mr. Kratsios holds a Bachelor of Arts in politics but no degrees in science or engineering.

Title: Previously Approved NSF Grants Paused Pending Additional Review
Summary: The National Science Foundation’s division of grants and awards is returning previously approved grant proposals for a “secondary review” to ensure funded projects align with President Trump’s priorities.

Title: Professor Directed by Florida State Government to Make Changes in Science Textbooks
Summary: Biology professor and textbook author Ken Miller was instructed by Florida state officials to remove certain references to “climate change” from a version of a textbook he co-authored which is to be used in Florida public schools.

Title: Proposal to Defund State Research Centers Advanced by Iowa Legislature
Summary: The Iowa legislature published its proposed state education budget for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2017. Under the proposal, state funding for education would be cut by $20 million, compared to the 2016 fiscal year. The cuts would be achieved, in part, by eliminating funding for two research centers at Iowa State University.

Title: Proposal to Defund Wyoming Environmental Quality Council Approved by State House of Representatives
Summary: The Wyoming House of Representatives voted to withhold funding from the state’s Environmental Quality Council. The vote was reportedly prompted by a concern that the Council, which reviews matters relating to environmental pollution, is too “anti-business.”

Title: Proposed Montana Bill Would Limit Science Education to “Scientific Fact”
Summary: A proposed bill in Montana would limit science education to “scientific fact” in the most “strictly enforced and narrowly interpreted” sense. Critics of the bill say that this would inhibit not only instruction about climate change, but wide swaths of school science curricula, such as gravity and cell theory.

Title: Proven Scientific Theories Questioned by Alabama Legislature
Summary: The Alabama legislature passed a resolution labeling climate change, evolution, and human cloning as “controversial” theories and encouraging teachers to question their accuracy.

Title: Public Communication by DOI Staff Restricted
Summary: The Trump transition team instructed DOI staff not to send out press releases, post on social media or blogs, or update websites and to consult with senior officials before speaking to the media.

Title: Public Communication by Government Health Officials Restricted
Summary: Vice President Mike Pence directed members of the White House coronavirus task force to coordinate all public appearances and statements through his office. Subsequent media reports indicate that the directive was intended to prevent public health officials and scientists making what the Trump administration saw as "alarmist messages" about the COVID-19 pandemic.

Title: Public Communication by HHS Staff Restricted
Summary: The Trump transition team instructed HHS staff not to send out press releases, post on social media or blogs, or update websites and to consult with senior officials before speaking to the media.

Title: Public Communication by NPS Scientists Restricted
Summary: A survey of scientists at DOI’s National Park Service found various restrictions on their ability to communicate with the public and journalists.

Title: Public Communication by USDA Staff Restricted
Summary: The Trump transition team instructed USDA staff not to send out press releases, post on social media or blogs, or update websites and to consult with senior officials before speaking to the media.

Title: Public Communication by USGS Scientists Restricted
Summary: DOI issued a new media protocol that requires scientists at the U.S. Geological Survey to obtain approval before speaking to journalists. The protocol permits DOI to reject interview requests on scientific matters.

Title: Public Health Officials Forced to Leave Positions
Summary: Backlash against public health officials seeking to enforce measures to curb the spread of COVID-19 have led 181 top state and local health officials to leave their posts since April 1, 2020, according to a joint study by the Associated Press and KHN.

Title: Publication of Energy Market Analyses Cancelled by DOE
Summary: The Deputy Secretary of Energy Mark Menezes reportedly ordered the Department of Energy's Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy to stop publishing quarterly and annual renewable energy market updates. Going forward, the updates will only be published every second year.

Title: Rand Paul Miscommunicates COVID Immunity
Summary: During a television interview, Senator Rand Paul erroneously said that individuals who have recovered from COVID-19 “are now immune,” and should “throw away their masks, go to restaurants, live again.”

Title: References “Climate Change” Removed from FEMA Documents
Summary: The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) published a new strategic plan in which all references to “climate change” and “global warming” have been removed.

Title: References to “Clean Energy” Removed from DOE Website
Summary: DOE’s “Clean Energy Investment Center” was renamed the “Energy Investor Center” and all references to “clean energy” were removed from its website.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from Amtrak Strategic Plan
Summary: Amtrak published a new five strategic plan, which omitted any reference to climate change. Previous versions of the plan had listed “environment / climate change” as the number one threat to Amtrak’s system.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from CDC Website
Summary: In late 2016, following the presidential election, several references to “climate change” were removed from the website of the CDC’s National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from DOD Report
Summary: A DOD report discussing how climate change is affecting military bases was edited to remove several references to “climate change” and downplay its impacts. For example, several references to climate change-induced sea level rise and its impact on military bases were removed, as were references to declines in Arctic sea ice.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from DOE Documents
Summary: Internal DOE documents were amended to re-frame “climate change” projects as “weather” or “jobs” projects.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from DOI Strategic Plan
Summary: The Department of the Interior (DOI) published a new five-year strategic plan which omitted any reference to “climate change.” The previous version of the plan had included a detailed discussion of the likely impacts of climate change on DOI’s activities and required the department to take steps to mitigate those impacts.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from EPA Document
Summary: The White House Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs deleted several references to “climate change” from an EPA guidance document titled “Planning for Natural Disaster Debris.” The deleted references highlighted the link between climate change and extreme weather events.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from EPA Document
Summary: The White House Office of Management and Budget removed several paragraphs discussing climate change and its impact on human health from a proposed EPA rule. The removed paragraphs emphasized the particular vulnerability of “children, the elderly, and the poor . . . to climate-related health effects.”

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from EPA Regulatory Impact Analysis
Summary: The White House Office of Management and Budget removed several paragraphs discussing climate change and its impact on human health from EPA’s regulatory impact analysis on the proposed Affordable Clean Energy Rule.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from EPA’s International Website
Summary: Between October and December 2017, EPA made various changes to its International Cooperation website, including removing information about climate change.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from FHWA Program
Summary: The Federal Highway Administration’s “Sustainable Transport and Climate Change” program was renamed the “Sustainable Transportation and Resilience” program and several references to climate change were removed from its website.

Title: References to "Climate Change" Removed from Forest Service Document
Summary: An official at the Forest Service ordered staff to remove all references to "climate change" and "greenhouse gases" from a regulatory document noticing the preparation of an environmental impact statement analyzing the effects of leasing certain national forest land for oil and gas development.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from Idaho Science Education Standards
Summary: The Idaho House of Representatives Education Committee voted to remove references to “climate change” from draft science education standards.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from NIH Website
Summary: References to “climate change” and links to climate-related documents, including an educational fact sheet on climate change and human health, were removed from the National Environmental Health Sciences website.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from NPS Report
Summary: A draft planning document, developed for the New Bedford Whaling Historical Park, was edited to remove all references to “climate change.” The edits were made after officials in the National Park Service’s Northeast Regional Office warned park employees that “we’re being told . . . [to] avoid” discussion of future climate impacts.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from Report of Vermont Natural Resources Board
Summary: The Vermont Governor’s office removed several references to “climate change” from a report, prepared by the state Natural Resources Board, exploring changes to the state’s land use permitting process.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from Treasury Department Report
Summary: References to “climate change” were removed from the Department of the Treasury’s 2017 Sustainability Report. In a departure from previous versions, the 2017 report no longer lists “climate change resilience” as a goal of the department, and omits discussion of programs aimed at addressing climate change risks.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from USAID Report
Summary: References to “climate change” were removed from the U.S. Agency for International Development’s 2017 Sustainability Report. In a departure from previous versions, the 2017 report no longer lists “climate change adaptation” as a priority, and omits a section discussing “proposed actions” to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

Title: References to "Climate Change" Removed from USGS Press Release
Summary: USGS revised a researcher-written news release on a study of the California coastline to omit all reference to the impact of climate change on the coastline.

Title: References to “Climate Change” Removed from Wisconsin Department of Natural Resources’ Website
Summary: The Wisconsin Department of Natural Resources’ website was changed to downplay the scientific consensus on climate change. Content describing the human activity as the main cause of climate change and the adverse effects of climate change were changed to state that the causes and effects “are being debated.”

Title: References to “Evolution” Removed from Arizona Education Standards
Summary: The Arizona Department of Education revised draft science education standards to limit the teaching of evolution and give teachers greater flexibility to present it as a controversial topic about which scientists disagree.

Title: References to Humans’ Role in Climate Change Removed from NPS Report
Summary: Officials at the National Parks Service edited a report on the impact of sea level rise and flooding on coastal national parks to remove all references to humans’ role in causing climate change.

Title: Release of CDC Report Blocked
Summary: Officials at the Centers for Disease Control and White House blocked release of a report, developed by infectious disease experts, to guide community leaders on how to safely reopen when COVID-19 lockdown measures are lifted.

Title: Release of Chemical Assessments Blocked by EPA
Summary: EPA officials told staff in the Integrated Risk Information System program that they could only release chemical assessments that had been specifically requested by an EPA program office, which was instructed to limit their assessment requests to “no more than three to four chemicals.”

Title: Release of Climate Change Study Blocked by Amtrak
Summary: Amtrak blocked release of a study, titled “Amtrak NEC Climate Change Vulnerability Assessment,” detailing the impact of climate change on a 10-mile stretch of the Northeast Corridor Route connecting Boston to Washington D.C. A redacted version of the study was released, pursuant to a Freedom of Information Act request, in November 2018.

Title: Release of Congressionally-Mandated Study Blocked by DOE
Summary: Political appointees at the Department of Energy blocked release of a congressionally-mandated study of the effects of adopting a national zero-emissions energy credit program.

Title: Release of COVID-19 Study Delayed by CDC and White House Officials
Summary: Officials at the White House and Centers for Disease Control blocked release of a study finding that the promotion of mask wearing by public officials is likely to lead to public adoption of the behavior. The study reportedly spent months under review "with no clear explanation," before being published days after President Trump first publicly wore a mask.

Title: Release of Electric Grid Study Blocked by DOE
Summary: The Department of Energy blocked publication of the findings of the Interconnections Seam Study, which evaluated the benefits of linking the eastern and western electric grid interconnections.

Title: Release of FDA Vaccine Guidelines Blocked by White House
Summary: White House chief of staff Mark Meadows reportedly blocked the release of guidelines by the Food and Drug Administration outlining requirements for the emergency authorization of a COVID-19 vaccine. Mr. Meadows objected to a requirement that, before any vaccine is authorized, volunteers who have participated in vaccine clinical trials be monitored for a median of two months after their final dose.

Title: Release of Federal Sustainability Reports Delayed
Summary: The White House Office of Management and Budget has delayed the release of sustainability reports prepared by at least seven federal agencies under Executive Order 13693 (signed by President Obama in March 2015).

Title: Release of Health Study Blocked by EPA
Summary: Senior EPA officials reported sought to block release of a scientific study discussing the health hazards posed by formaldehyde.

Title: Release of Hydropower Studies Blocked by DOE
Summary: Political appointees at the Department of Energy blocked release of up to six studies commissioned by the Water Power Technologies Office. The release of one study, which evaluated the potential for using hydropower to support solar and wind generation, was delayed by over a year.

Title: Release of NOAA / NASA Temperature Data Delayed
Summary: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration and the National Aeronautics and Space Administration have been forced to delay release of the annual global temperature analysis for 2018 due to the partial government shutdown.

Title: Release of NOAA Disaster-Cost Estimate Delayed
Summary: Due to the partial government shutdown, the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration has delayed release of its annual disaster-cost estimate, showing the total cost of weather and climate-related disasters in 2018.

Title: Release of Pesticide Study Blocked by DOI
Summary: Political appointees at the Department of the Interior blocked the release of a report, prepared by scientists at DOI’s Fish and Wildlife Service, that found that use of the pesticides would threaten the continued existence of thousands of species.

Title: Release of Scientific Model Delayed Due to Partial Government Shutdown
Summary: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration was forced to delay release of an update to the World Magnetic Model due to the partial government shutdown.

Title: Release of Scientific Study Blocked
Summary: White House and EPA officials sought to block publication of HHS study on the health effects of water contamination due to concerns that it would be a “public relations nightmare.”

Title: Release of Solar Energy Studies Blocked by DOE
Summary: Political appointees at the Department of Energy (DOE) blocked release of at least twenty-four reports commissioned by the Solar Energy Technologies Office. At least two of the studies had been under review by DOE's Deputy Assistant Secretary David Solan for seventeen months.

Title: Release of USGS Study Delayed
Summary: The director of the U.S. Geological Survey James Reilly blocked the release of a peer-reviewed, internally approved study on the impact of oil and gas development on polar bear populations in Alaska for at least three months.

Title: Reliability of Climate Models Misrepresented by EPA Administrator
Summary: During an interview with the Brazilian news outlet Folha de S.Paulo, EPA Administrator Andrew Wheeler erroneously claimed that a climate scenario commonly used in scientific assessments to model the impact of high greenhouse gas emissions is "not reliable," and amounts to "fear propaganda."

Title: Report Reveals Faulty Science Behind Alabama Criminalization of Transgender Medical Treatment
Summary: On April 28, 2022, Yale and the University of Texas Southwestern published a report revealing the faulty science behind Alabama’s state law criminalizing transgender medical care, “Biased Science: The Texas and Alabama Measures Criminalizing Medical Treatment for Transgender Children and Adolescents Rely on Inaccurate and Misleading Scientific Statements.”

Title: Report Reveals Faulty Science Behind Texas Criminalization of Transgender Medical Treatment
