Summary: On April 28, 2022, Yale and the University of Texas Southwestern published a report revealing the faulty science behind the Texas Governor’s criminalization of transgender medical care, “Biased Science: The Texas and Alabama Measures Criminalizing Medical Treatment for Transgender Children and Adolescents Rely on Inaccurate and Misleading Scientific Statements.”

Title: Representative Marjorie Taylor Greene Shares Misleading Graph to Bolster Support for Fossil Fuels
Summary: On April 15, 2023, Georgia Representative Marjorie Taylor Greene tweeted that carbon emissions do not cause climate change, and that fossil fuels are “natural and amazing.” She provided a graph showing a decrease in certain air pollutants since the 1970s, but the graph did not include carbon dioxide or methane emissions – the major contributors to global warming.

Title: Research Findings Questioned by New Hampshire Cities
Summary: The cities of Dover, Portsmouth, and Rochester in New Hampshire questioned the accuracy of scientific research finding that municipal wastewater is contributing to high nitrogen levels in the Great Bay estuary.

Title: Research Funding Cut by Florida Citrus Department
Summary: The Florida Department of Citrus cut $70,000 from its scientific research, marketing, and administration budgets. The cuts were reportedly necessary due to lower than expected revenues.

Title: Research Funding Reallocated by HHS
Summary: HHS announced that it would use funds originally allocated to research and other programs to cover the costs of its Unaccompanied Alien Children program. A total of $266 million will be re-diverted, including $87.3 million originally allocated to the National Institutes of Health for cancer and other research.

Title: Research Funding Withheld by DOE
Summary: An investigation by the Government Accountability Office (GAO) found that the Department of Energy's research and innovation arm -- the Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (ARPA-E) -- had inappropriately withheld $91 million of appropriated funds in Fiscal Year 2017.

Title: Research Grants Cancelled by HHS
Summary: HHS cancelled $213.6 million in funding for teen pregnancy prevention programs and research. The cancellation will affect five-year grants awarded to 80 institutions by the Obama administration.

Title: Research Grants Suspended by EPA
Summary: On December 20, 2017, Trump administration official ordered the Environmental Protection Agency to suspend all research grants.

Title: Research into Industrial Hemp Prevented by Nevada Governor
Summary: Nevada Governor Susana Martinez vetoed a bill authorizing the New Mexico Department of Agriculture to adopt rules for research into industrial hemp. As a result of the veto, research into industrial hemp remains prohibited in Nevada.

Title: Research into Sea-Ice Cover Obstructed by Congress
Summary: Congress ordered the dismantling of a back-up satellite needed to investigate the impact of climate change on sea-ice in the poles.

Title: Research Study Paused by Army Corps of Engineers
Summary: The U.S. Army Corps of Engineer paused a six-year study of possible measures to reduce flood risk in New York and New Jersey. The Corps indicated that the study "did not receive federal appropriation funding" in the agency's 2020 work plan, but did not explain why that was the case.

Title: Researchers Funded by Department of Education Ordered Not to Publish Research Results Without Prior Written Approval
Summary: Academic researchers and contractors with the Department of Education were ordered not to publish any research findings or information without written approval from the agency’s press secretary.

Title: Resolution Undermining Science Education Passed by Indiana Senate
Summary: The Indiana Senate passed a resolution urging support for the teaching of a diverse curriculum. Critics say this will undermine the integrity of science education, including by encouraging teachers to present a range of views on topics seen as controversial, such as evolution.

Title: Resolution Undermining Teaching of Climate Change Introduced in South Dakota
Summary: A resolution (HCR 1002) was introduced in the South Dakota legislature that would, if adopted, encourage each local school board to adopt rules preventing teachers “[a]dvocat[ing] for any issue that is part of a political party platform.” Critics fear that this could limit teachers’ ability to discuss climate change, which is dealt with in the Democratic party platform.

Title: Resolution Undermining Teaching of Climate Change Introduced in Virginia
Summary: A resolution (HJ 684) was introduced in the Virginia legislature that would, if adopted, request that the State Board of Education adopt rules preventing teachers “[a]dvocat[ing] for any issue that is part of a political party platform.” Critics fear that this could limit teachers’ ability to discuss climate change, which is dealt with in the Democratic party platform.

Title: RFK Jr. Makes False Claims about MMR Vaccine
Summary: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. falsely stated that the measles, mumps, and rubella (MMR) vaccine has not been “safety tested” and that it offered only short-term protection against measles.

Title: RFK, Jr. Cancels U.S. Preventive Services Task Force Meeting
Summary: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. abruptly and without explanation postponed a meeting of the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force scheduled for the following day.

Title: RFK, Jr. Dismisses Entire Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices
Summary: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. dismissed all 17 members of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP), describing the committee as "plagued by conflicts of interest."

Title: School District in Texas Votes to Remove Chapters from Science Textbooks
Summary: Trustees for the Cy-Fair ISD voted to remove chapters from science textbooks that address subjects like climate change and vaccinations.

Title: Science of Evolution Questioned by Arizona State Official
Summary: In a television interview, Arizona Superintendent of Public Instruction Diane Douglas suggested that science only “back[s] up part of” the theory of evolution, and “not all of it.” Superintendent Douglas has previously advocated for Florida schools to teach the unproven theory of “intelligent design” as an alternative to evolution.

Title: Science of Evolution Questioned by Utah Education Board Member
Summary: During a hearing of the Utah State Board of Education, vice-chairwoman Alisa Ellis argued that teachers should present evolution as a theory and not a fact. She also urged teachers to discuss alternative explanations for the origins of life, including the unproven theory of “intelligent design.”

Title: Science of Immunization Misrepresented by Kentucky Governor
Summary: During a media interview, Kentucky Governor Matt Bevin misrepresented the science of infectious diseases, presenting “chickenpox on purpose” as a valid alternative to vaccination. That view is not shared by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, which recommends that children receive the chickenpox vaccination, and not be purposely exposed to the disease.

Title: Science of Immunization Misrepresented in Congressional Hearing
Summary: During a Congressional hearing, Senator Rand Paul erroneously suggested that the benefits of immunization have not been scientifically proven.

Title: Science of Vaccination Misrepresented by Louisiana State Senator
Summary: Louisiana State Senator John Milkovich claimed that vaccination can cause Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) and bowel disease.

Title: Science of Vaccination Misrepresented by Tennessee Congressman-Elect and Former State Senator
Summary: Tennessee Congressman-elect and former Senator Mark Green appeared to suggest that vaccination can cause Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD). The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has proven that there is no link between receiving vaccines and developing ASD.

Title: Science of Viral Infection Treatment Misrepresented by Texas State Representative
Summary: Texas State Representative Bill Zedler falsely stated that measles, a viral infection, can be cured by antibiotics which exclusively treat bacterial infections.

Title: Science on Vaccine Safety Misrepresented by Milwaukee Health Department Official
Summary: During a radio interview, the newly appointed head of Milwaukee’s Health Department, Patricia McManus, stated that “the science is still out” on whether vaccines cause autism. Any link between vaccines and autism has, however, been disproved in numerous scientific studies.

Title: Scientific Advice on Chemical Disregarded by EPA
Summary: EPA denied a petition to ban Chlorpyrifos, a chemical widely used as a pesticide, against the advice of top agency scientists who had warned that exposure exceeds safe levels in many areas. [Reversed by Biden administration]

Title: Scientific Advisory Committee’s Advice Disregarded in Federal Dietary Guidelines
Summary: The 2020-2025 edition of the Dietary Guidelines for Americans, issued by the U.S. Department of Agriculture and Department of Health and Human Services, did not include limits on sugar and alcohol intake recommended by a scientific advisory committee commissioned by the same federal agencies to advise on the content of the guidelines.

Title: Scientific Conference Cancelled by USDA Due to Partial Government Shutdown
Summary: Citing the partial federal government shutdown, the U.S. Department of Agriculture cancelled its 30th annual Research Forum on Invasive Species, which had been scheduled to take place from January 8 to 11. According to USDA officials, the forum will not be rescheduled during 2019, due to difficulties “coordinating with speakers and the hotel.”

Title: Scientific Consensus on Climate Change Questioned by President Trump
Summary: While being briefed on the wildfires spreading across the western United States, President Trump questioned the scientific consensus that climate change has contributed to a worsening of fires, saying “I don’t think science knows.”

Title: Scientific Consensus on Climate Change Questioned by President Trump
Summary: During a televised interview, President Trump questioned the scientific consensus on climate change, suggesting that scientists disagree on its causes. He also asserted that some scientists have overstated the role human activities play in climate change, saying “[w]e do have an impact but I don’t believe the impact is nearly what some scientists say.”

Title: Scientific Consensus on Climate Change Questioned by Wyoming State Representative
Summary: Wyoming State Representative Scott Clem asserted, on his website, that “man-made climate change is not settled science” and continues to be “hotly disputed.”

Title: Scientific Consensus on Climate Change Rejected by Kentucky Governor
Summary: During an interview on Cincinnati radio, Kentucky Governor Matt Bevin accepted that the climate “has been changing,” but suggested this is a natural phenomenon. Governor Bevin described scientific research showing that human activities contribute to climate change as “ludicrous” and “absolutely preposterous.”

Title: Scientific Consensus on Climate Change Rejected by Louisiana Attorney General
Summary: During a press conference, Louisiana Attorney General Jeff Landry described climate change as a “hoax,” and denied that it is causing sea level rise. Later on the same day, the Attorney General published a pair of tweets, asserting that human activities do not cause climate change and that sea levels are not rising.

Title: Scientific Data and Records to be Destroyed by DOI
Summary: DOI filed a “Request for Records Disposition Authority” with the National Archives and Records Administration (NARA). In the request, DOI seeks permission from NARA to destroy records relating to its conservation, land use, energy, and water programs. Many of the records document the findings of scientific research or contain data needed to conduct such research.

Title: Scientific Findings of Environment Agency Questioned by Ohio Governor
Summary: Ohio Governor John Kasich questioned scientific data showing poor water quality in Lake Erie. Based on the data, the Ohio Environmental Protection Agency concluded that the lake’s waters are impaired, but the Governor disputed that finding.

Title: Scientific Information Ignored by EPA in MATS Review
Summary: A scientific review concluded that EPA's finding that the Mercury and Air Toxics Standards are not "appropriate and necessary" was based on a flawed analysis that ignored research on the effects of mercury emissions on public health.

Title: Scientific Report Revised by Political Appointees at DOI
Summary: Scientists at the Department of the Interior prepared a report which shows that a Trump administration plan to allocate additional water to farmers in California’s central valley would endanger salmon and whale populations. The Trump administration subsequently ordered a “strike team” to re-write the report without any reference to the risk to salmon and whales.

Title: Scientific Research on Cancer Risk Misrepresented
Summary: Retailers in California were required to include a cancer warning on coffee, despite scientific evidence showing that it does not pose a cancer risk.

Title: Scientific Research Subject to Political Interference at EPA
Summary: A government survey of EPA employees found evidence of political interference in scientific research. Almost 400 employees "involved in science" said they had experienced, but did not report, potential violations of EPA's scientific integrity policy.

Title: Scientific Research Subject to Political Interference at NOAA
Summary: A survey of federal government scientists found evidence of political interference in scientific research at the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration. The survey found, among other things, that scientists have been told to avoid research linking environmental problems to action by industry.

Title: Scientist Fired from Black Hawk College
Summary: Black Hawk College announced it would lay off several faculty members, including a professor of geology, due to a lack of state funding.

Title: Scientist Forced to Suspend Water Testing Due to Government Shutdown
Summary: A scientist from Virginia Commonwealth University was prevented from collecting water samples in the Shenandoah National Park due to the partial government shutdown. Prior to the shutdown, samples had been collected weekly as part of a forty-year study, intended to track how streams in the park and surrounding areas are recovering from acidification.

Title: Scientists Repeatedly Ignored in Alaska Oil Drilling Environmental Review Process
Summary: 1.6 million acres of the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge in Alaska was opened up for oil drilling. In the rush to complete the environmental review required before leasing can begin, scientists have been repeatedly ignored and sidelined. Among other things, DOI failed to consider or publish 18 export-written memos on critical information gaps in its draft Environmental Impact Statements.

Title: Scientists' Advice on COVID-19 Ignored by White House Officials
Summary: Throughout January 2020, government scientists and heath experts repeatedly warned White House officials of the risks posed by COVID-19, and urged action to limit the spread of the disease. The warnings were dismissed by officials as "alarmist."

Title: Second Bill Requiring Use of “Balanced” Teaching Materials Introduced in Florida
Summary: A bill was introduced in Florida that would, if enacted, require the instructional materials used in public schools to “provide objective, balances, and non-inflammatory viewpoints on controversial issues.” The term “controversial issues” is not defined but may encompass climate change and evolution.

Title: Secretary of Energy Erroneously Claims There Are “Pluses to Global Warming”
Summary: The newly confirmed Secretary of Energy, Chris Wright, stated in an interview with Fox Business that “there’s pluses to global warming as well as negatives,” and made factually incorrect claims about positive impacts of climate change.

Title: Senator and Vice President-Elect JD Vance Calls Climate Change “Weird Science”
Summary: In the 2024 vice presidential debate, Senator JD Vance questioned the science of climate change.

Title: Senators Request Review of NSF Grants
Summary: Four Republican Senators requested that the Inspector General of the National Science Foundation (NSF) commence a review of its grant-making process. The Senators allege that NSF has issued several grants in violation of federal law, including one supporting a program intended to educate meteorologists about climate change.

Title: Standards Undermining Science Education Proposed by New Mexico Education Department
Summary: The New Mexico Public Education Department proposed to adopt the Next Generation Science Standards, with various amendments, which critics say will limit discussion of climate change and evolution in schools.

Title: State Department Scientist Prevented from Submitting Written Testimony to Congress
Summary: White House officials ordered a senior scientist at the U.S. Department of State not to submit written testimony on the threats posed by climate change to the House Intelligence Committee.

Title: Stem Cell Research Funding Cut from NY State Budget
Summary: The New York state legislature approved a 2022 budget which eliminated the New York State Stem Cell Science program. The budget, signed by Governor Andrew Cuomo, takes effect on May 1 and will halt funding for new grants within the program while honoring all existing contracts.

Title: Studies Showing Damages from Climate Change Buried by USDA
Summary: Scientists at the USDA claim that the department has deliberately failed to publicize government-funded research publications that address the impact of climate change.

Title: Survey of EPA Scientists Reveals “Culture of Fear”
Summary: An internal survey of EPA scientists designed to assess management and workplace culture in order to “enhance the science” reveals a “culture of fear,” retaliation, and hostility.

Title: Teaching of Evolution Undermined in Louisiana
Summary: The Louisiana Board of Elementary and Secondary Education voted to amend a guidance document on the state’s science education standards. The amendment referenced the Louisiana Science Education Act, which encourages the teaching of alternatives to evolution.

Title: Teaching of Unproven Scientific Concept Supported by Arizona State Official
Summary: At a Republican candidate event, Arizona Superintendent of Public Instruction Diane Douglas argued that school children should be taught alternatives to the theory of evolution, including the unproven theory of “intelligent design.”

Title: Tennessee Passes a Bill Redefining Natural Gas as “Clean Energy”
Summary: Tennessee Governor Bill Lee has signed a bill that redefines methane gas as a source of clean energy equivalent to wind, solar, and hydroelectric power.

Title: Texas Board of Education Weakens Guidelines Surrounding Climate Education
Summary: The Texas Board of Education changed their operating rules to steer schools towards curricula that refrains from criticizing fossil fuels and gives weight to the debunked argument that climate change is largely the result of natural cycles, not human activity.

Title: EPA Suppressed Scientists’ Concerns About the PolyMet Copper-Nickel Mine in Minnesota
Summary: Scientists at the Environmental Protection Agency expressed concern that the PolyMet copper-nickel mine in Minnesota would violate the Clean Water Act, but, in a highly unusual move, the scientists' written comments were never included in the public written record.

Title: Thousands of CDC, NIH Employees Fired
Summary: The Trump administration fired thousands of Health and Human Services employees, including 1,300 from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and 1,500 from the National Institutes of Health.

Title: Three EPA Advisory Committees Disbanded
Summary: EPA’s Science Advisory Board (SAB) announced that it was disbanding three sub-committees — the Ecological Processes and Effects Committee, Environmental Engineering Committee, and Environmental Economics Advisory Committee — “[o]n the recommendation of the SAB Staff Office.”

Title: Trump Administration “Indefinitely” Blocks Submissions to the Federal Register
Summary: The Trump administration has blocked National Institutes of Health submissions to the Federal Register “indefinitely,” preventing the disbursement of billions of dollars in grant funding.

Title: Trump Administration Abruptly Suspends All Travel by National Institutes of Health Employees
Summary: On January 22, 2025, employees at the National Institutes of Health were informed that all travel, foreign and domestic, has been suspended immediately and indefinitely.

Title: Trump Administration Axes Federal Advisory Committee at the Department of Commerce
Summary: NOAA’s Marine Protected Areas Federal Advisory Committee was discontinued at the direction of the Trump administration.

Title: Trump Administration Axes Federal Advisory Committee Focused on Invasive Species
Summary: The Department of the Interior’s Invasive Species Advisory Committee was discontinued at the direction of the Trump administration.

Title: Trump Administration Cancels $4 Million for Climate Research at Princeton University
Summary: Commerce Department Secretary Howard Lutnick announced that the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) is ending nearly $4 million in funding to Princeton University for climate research.

Title: Trump Administration Cancels Contract for Bird Flu Vaccine Development
Summary: The Trump administration canceled a $590 million contract with Moderna supporting the development of a bird flu vaccine for humans and expanded clinical studies for additional pandemic influenza subtypes.

Title: Trump Administration Cancels Federal Funding for Harvard School of Public Health
Summary: On May 15, 2025, more than 130 researchers at the Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health were notified that federal funding for their work was being cancelled, following Harvard's refusal to comply with a list of demands from the Trump administration.

Title: Trump Administration Cancels Funding for Women’s Health Initiative
Summary: The National Institutes of Health’s Women’s Health Initiative announced that the contracts supporting its four regional centers will be terminated and that the program’s clinical coordinating center “will continue operations until January 2026, after which time its funding remains uncertain.”

Title: Trump Administration Closes 25 USGS Water Science Centers
Summary: The Trump administration has ordered the closure of 25 United States Geological Service water science centers after the “Department of Government Efficiency” terminated the centers’ leases.

Title: Trump Administration Cuts $4 Billion from Medical Research Funding
Summary: The Trump administration is cutting $4 billion in medical research funding by limiting the amount of grant money universities, hospitals, and other institutions receive for indirect costs associated with scientific research.

Title: Trump Administration Cuts Conservation Research Funding
Summary: As a result of budget cuts by the Trump Administration, 16 out of 22 Land Conservation Cooperative research centers have been placed on indefinite hiatus or completely eliminated.

Title: Trump Administration Cuts Funding for EPA Office of Research and Development
Summary: The Trump administration halted funding for the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Office of Research and Development (ORD), the agency’s main science division.

Title: Trump Administration Deals Potentially Fatal Blow to National Climate Assessment
Summary: The Trump administration cancelled its contract with the consulting firm that produces the US Global Change Research Program (USGCRP).

Title: Trump Administration Fails to Appoint Science Advisor
Summary: As of October 23, 2017, the Trump administration had taken longer than any other to nominate a science adviser to head the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy.

Title: Trump Administration Fails to Fill Science Positions
Summary: As of December 31, 2017, President Trump had filled fewer government science positions than any other recent President, at the same point in time. Just 20 of the 83 positions designated by the National Academies of Sciences as “science appointees” had been filled.

Title: Trump Administration Freezes Billions of Dollars in Federal Grants and Loans
Summary: On January 28, 2025, the White House Office of Management and Budget ordered federal agencies to stop disbursing funds under federal grant and loan programs. Among the affected programs are grants issued by the National Science Foundation.
Update Status: Additional Anti-Science Action|Reversed (Partially or Fully)|Litigation Update

Title: Trump Administration Freezes Spending on Government-Issued Credit Cards
Summary: The Trump administration issued an executive order freezing spending on government-issued credit cards for 30 days.

Title: Trump Administration Halts External Communications by Health and Human Services Staff
Summary: On January 22, 2025, the Trump administration ordered a pause on all external communications by employees of the Department of Health and Human Services and its sub-agencies.

Title: Trump Administration Indefinitely Cancels Federal Advisory Committee Meetings
Summary: The Trump administration cancelled federal scientific advisory committee meetings at several federal agencies, with no word on when or if the meetings would resume.

Title: Trump Administration Limiting Scientific Input in Formulation of the USDA’s 2020 Dietary Guidelines
Summary: The Trump administration is limiting the scope of scientific input into the USDA’s 2020 dietary guidelines.

Title: Trump Administration Officials Ordered Distribution of Millions of Doses of Hydroxychloroquine, Bypassing Mandatory FDA Safety Controls
Summary: The Trump administration bypassed safety controls at the Food and Drug Administration (FDA), ordering the distribution of tens of millions of doses of hydroxychloroquine to be used as a "potential cure" for Covid.

Title: Trump Administration Purges Federal Heat Experts
Summary: Top heat experts in the U.S. government have been fired, resigned, or accepted deferred resignation offers, including the entire staff at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention's climate office.

Title: Trump Administration Terminates National Nature Assessment Weeks Before Publication
Summary: The Trump administration terminated the National Nature Assessment, a forthcoming first-of-its-kind report on the state of America’s land, water, and wildlife.

Title: Trump Administration Withdraws NIH Scientific Integrity Policy
Summary: The Trump administration formally rescinded the National Institutes of Health (NIH) scientific integrity policy, which had been created to protect federal scientists from political interference and retaliation.

Title: Trump Appointee Sought to Prevent Anthony Fauci Speaking to Media About the Risks Coronavirus Poses to Children
Summary: In September 2020, a Trump administration appointee to the Department of Health and Human Servics (HHS) attempted to prevent Anthony Fauci, the nation’s top infectious disease expert, from speaking to media about the risks coronavirus poses to children.

Title: Trump Appointee to the Department of Health and Human Services Directed Agency Staff Not to Speak to Media About the Coronavirus Pandemic
Summary: In July 2020, Michael Caputo, the assistant secretary for public affairs at the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) warned agency staff against speaking with media about the coronavirus pandemic.

Title: Trump Appointees to Centers for Disease Control and Prevention Have No Public Health Background, Clear Roles at Agency
Summary: In June 2020, Trump appointed two individuals without public health backgrounds to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)’s offices in Atlanta, without informing senior agency staff as to what their roles would be.

Title: Trump Cancels EPA Scientific Advisors Meeting
Summary: On April 15, 2025, the Trump administration canceled a meeting of top scientific advisors to the Environmental Protection Agency.

Title: Trump Officials Downplayed Findings in USGS Climate Change Study
Summary: A press release on a US Geological Survey (USGS) study on the cost of climate change on California’s economy was edited by then USGS director James Reilly. Prior to review by USGS officials, the draft press release detailed over $100 billion of climate-related damages. The final version made no mention of monetary damages and instead publicized “state-of-the-art” computer models used in the study.

Title: Trump Proposes $1.7 Billion Cut to NOAA
Summary: The Trump administration has proposed a $1.7 billion cut to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA)’s 2026 budget and the elimination of nearly all NOAA research labs.

Title: Trump Restricts Gain-of-Function Research
Summary: President Trump signed an executive order halting "gain-of-function research" on viruses. The administration suggested such research is “dangerous” and referenced the controversial theory that the COVID-19 virus originated from a lab in China that employed similar research methods.

Title: Trump Science Nominees Lack Qualifications
Summary: Of 43 Trump administration nominees in science-related positions, only 41% had a master’s degree or doctorate in a science field, compared to 58% under President Obama.

Title: Two Bills in Oklahoma Promote Teaching of Intelligent Design
Summary: Two bills were introduced in the Oklahoma House of Representatives that would allow teachers to teach and discuss intelligent design, which the bills define as “a scientific theory.”

Title: Two DOE Advisory Committees Axed
Summary: The Department of Energy terminated the Technical Advisory Committee on Verification of Fissile Material and Nuclear Warhead Controls and the Hydrogen Technical Advisory Panel.

Title: Two Environmental Protection Agency Advisory Committees Disbanded
Summary: Members of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)’s Science Advisory Board (SAB) and the Clean Air Scientific Advisory Committee (CASAC) were informed that they had been removed from the committees.

Title: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service Official Pressured to Reverse Environmental Permit Decision
Summary: A former U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service official revealed that, while employed as a field supervisor in Arizona, he was pressured by the Trump Administration’s U.S. Department of the Interior to reverse his decision regarding a biological assessment of a proposed massive housing development project.

Title: U.S. Navy Shuts Task Force on Climate Change
Summary: The U.S. Navy has quietly shut down its Task Force on Climate Change

Title: Unqualified Individual Appointed to North Carolina Oil and Gas Commission
Summary: The North Carolina legislature appointed Jim Womak to the state’s Oil and Gas Commission. Mr. Womak was appointed to a seat reserved for a “representative of a nongovernmental conservation interest” but has no experience in conservation.

Title: Unqualified Individuals Appointed to Florida Wildlife Commission
Summary: Florida Governor Rick Scott appointed three individuals with little or no experience in wildlife management to the state’s Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission.

Title: Unqualified Individuals Appointed to Missouri Clean Water Commission
Summary: Missouri Governor Eric Greitens appointed three new members to the state’s Clean Water Commission. All three appointees have close ties to industry and appear to lack appropriate qualifications for serving on the Commission.

Title: USDA Budget Cuts Proposed for FY19
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2019 budget proposes to cut USDA funding by 16.4% (compared to FY2017 levels). Under the budget, funding for a number of USDA research programs, including statistical research programs administered by the Economic Research Service, would be reduced or eliminated.

Title: USDA Budget Cuts Proposed for FY20
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2020 budget proposes to cut USDA funding by 14.8% (compared to FY2019 levels). The cuts would affect several USDA research programs, including the Agricultural Research Service which would see its funding decline by 9.5%, and the National Agricultural Statistics Service which would see its funding decline by 14.7%.

Title: USDA Budget Cuts Proposed for FY2021
Summary: The Trump administration's FY2021 budget proposes to cut USDA funding by $16 billion or 9.9% (compared to FY2020 levels). The cuts would affect several USDA research programs, including the Agricultural Research Service which would see its funding decline by 11.7%, and the National Agricultural Statistics Service which would see its funding decline by 1.7%.

Title: USDA Cancels Hundreds of Scientific Journal Subscriptions
Summary: The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) canceled subscriptions to nearly 400 of the roughly 2,000 journals carried by the agency’s National Agricultural Library. This will limit access to the journals by USDA staff scientists and researchers at other institutions that do not have their own subscriptions.

Title: USDA Employees Ordered to Delete Web Pages Discussing Climate Change
Summary: Employees at the US Department of Agriculture (USDA) were ordered to delete landing pages that discussed climate change from agency websites and document all mentions of climate change for further review.

Title: USDA Officials Buried Climate Change Response Plan
Summary: The USDA buried a plan detailing how agriculture can adapt to climate change

Title: USDA Scientists Ordered to Label Peer-Reviewed Research “Preliminary”
Summary: Scientists at the U.S. Department of Agriculture were directed to include, on all research published in scientific journals, a statement indicating that “[t]he findings and conclusions in this preliminary publication have not been formally disseminated by the” department.

Title: USDA Scientists Prevented from Attending Scientific Conference
Summary: Hundreds of scientists from the U.S. Department of Agriculture were prevented from attending the 27th Annual Plant and Genome Conference due to the partial government shutdown.

Title: USDA Scientists Prevented from Researching “Politically Contentious” Topics
Summary: A survey of federal government scientists found evidence of political interference in scientific research at USDA. The survey found, among other things, that resources have been shifted away from USDA programs and offices involved in “politically contentious” research.

Title: USDA Staff Given List of 110 Banned Terms
Summary: Officials in the Agricultural Research Service (ARS) were sent a list of 110 banned words and phrases, including terms such as “clean water,” “climate change,” and “safe drinking water.”

Title: USDA Staff Told to Avoid References to “Climate Change”
Summary: USDA staff were instructed to avoid using certain terms, including “climate change,” which should be replaced with “weather extremes.”

Title: USDA’s FY18 Budget Cut by Trump Administration
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2018 budget proposed a 12% cut in funding for USDA climate and environmental research programs.

Title: Use of Science in EPA Air Pollution Programs Restricted
Summary: President Trump signed a memorandum directing the EPA Administrator to make changes to certain air pollution programs. The changes have implications for the use of science in developing and implementing air pollution protections.

Title: Use of Scientific Studies Restricted at DOI
Summary: The Deputy Secretary of the Department of the Interior, David Bernhardt, issued an order restricting the use of scientific evidence in departmental policy decisions.

Title: USFS Removes References to Climate Change from Wilderness Webpage
Summary: EDGI report shows that USFS removed references to climate change from its webpage about wilderness areas.

Title: USGCRP Removes Sections on Climate Change from its Website
Summary: EDGI report shows that USGCRP removed sections about climate change from its website

Title: USGS Analyst Falsified Water Quality Test Results
Summary: An analyst at the U.S. Geological Survey’s (USGS) National Water Quality Laboratory falsified thousands of water quality tests. The unnamed analyst reportedly falsified the tests to meet increasingly stringent deadlines and production expectations, citing “an immense feeling of pressure to get results out as quickly as possible” as motivating their action.

Title: USGS Budget Cuts Proposed for FY2026
Summary: The Trump administration’s FY2026 budget proposes to cut funding for the the U.S, Geological Survey by $564 million. The cuts specifically target climate change research and programs.

Title: USGS Lab Unjustly Fired Whistleblower Scientist
Summary: A scientist working in a Seattle-based U.S. Geological Survey lab documented and reported instances of lab misconduct. Her supervisors attempted to remove her from her post, but a judge ruled this a violation of the Whistleblower Protection Act.

Title: USGS Removes Climate Change Links and Page from its Website
Summary: EDGI report shows that the USGS removed all links and references to climate change from its Science Explorer section

Title: USGS Scientist Prevented from Publishing Research in Official Capacity
Summary: Dr. John Crusius, a research chemist at the U.S. Geological Survey, was directed not to disclose his government affiliation in a research paper he published on natural solutions to climate change.

Title: USGS Scientists Avoiding Discussion of “Climate Change”
Summary: A survey of scientists at the U.S. Geological Survey found that nearly one-third engage in self-censorship, avoiding discussion of, and work on, climate change even when not explicitly told to do so.

Title: USGS Scientists Barred from Conferences Unless Studies Found to Align with Administration's Values
Summary: The Department of Interior (DOI) posted new guidelines requiring US Geological Survey (USGS) scientists to be reviewed by political appointees if they wished to attend either the American Geophysical Union (AGU) or the Geological Society of America (GSA) conferences.

Title: USGS Scientists Ordered Not to Model Long-Term Climate Impacts
Summary: Scientists at the U.S. Geological Survey were directed to use computer-generated models that only project climate change impacts through 2040. The scientists had previously modeled climate change impacts through 2100, which experts say provides a more accurate picture of climate change because the effects of current greenhouse gas emissions will primarily be felt after 2040.

Title: Vice President Overruled CDC Scientists on Border Closure
Summary: Vice President Mike Pence ordered Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) Director Robert Redfield to utilize the authority granted to the CDC under the Public Health Services Act to take “extraordinary measures to limit transmission of an infectious disease" to close U.S. borders. The order was signed despite weeks of resistance from CDC scientists.

Title: Water Quality Records Destroyed by EPA
Summary: EPA destroyed 18 boxes of water quality records without notifying or obtaining approval from the National Archives and Records Administration as required by federal law.

Title: Water Testing Blocked by South Carolina Mayor
Summary: The mayor of Denmark in South Carolina, Gerald Wright, refused to allow a Virginia Tech professor, Marc Edwards, to test the city’s drinking water supplies for possible contamination. Mayor Wright asserted that the testing was unnecessary because the city’s water had already been assessed by the state Department of Health and Environmental Control and found to be safe.

Title: Websites for U.S. Regional Climate Centers Go Dark
Summary: On April 21, 2025, websites for U.S. regional climate centers went dark due to a funding delay within the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, halting public access to crucial climate data.

Title: Weekly CDC Report Resumes without Information on Bird Flu or mPox
Summary: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)’s Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report (MMWR) resumed after a two-week pause, but did not include information on bird flu or monkeypox.

Title: White House Blocked CDC Order Requiring Masks on Public and Commercial Transportation
Summary: The White House Coronavirus Task Force blocked publication of a Centers for Disease Control and Prevention order that would have required passengers and employees to wear facial coverings on public and commercial transportation nationwide to combat the spread of COVID-19.

Title: White House Chief of Staff Claims USDA Move Was Deliberate Attempt to Reduce Agency Workforce
Summary: Acting White House Chief of Staff Mick Mulvaney said that moving USDA groups out of DC to Kansas City was done to reduce the agency’s workforce.

Title: White House Forced EPA to Reject Scientific Findings Linking Chemical to Fetal Heart Defects
Summary: The Executive Office of the President directed EPA scientists to rewrite a draft assessment linking the chemical trichloroethylene (TCE) to fetal heart defects.

Title: White House Ordered Changes to CDC Coronavirus Guidelines
Summary: White House officials ordered changes to a guidance document published by the Centers for Disease Control (CDC) outlining measures that faith communities should take to mitigate the spread of coronavirus. The original version recommended that faith communities "consider suspending" choirs but White House officials directed the CDC to remove that recommendation.

Title: White House Strikes Climate Change Language from Vehicle Emissions Proposal
Summary: The White House deleted references to climate change from a proposal limiting California’s ability to set stricter vehicle emissions standards.

Title: White House’s Proposed FY2026 Budget Cuts NASA Funding
Summary: President Trump’s budget request for fiscal year 2026 proposes to cut the National Aeronautics and Space Administration’s overall budget by 20%, with cuts primarily affecting the Science Mission Directorate and its research in planetary science, Earth science, and astrophysics research.

Title: Wisconsin Department of Natural Resources’ Science Bureau Slated for Closure
Summary: In his budget proposal for 2017-19, Wisconsin Governor Scott Walker recommended eliminating the Department of Natural Resources’ central science services bureau, and reassigning its 15 staff to other positions.

Title: Wisconsin Natural Resources Magazine Slated for Closure
Summary: In his budget proposal for 2017-19, Wisconsin Governor Scott Walker recommended that the state Department of Natural Resources cease publishing a bimonthly magazine, providing information on environmental issues. Critics say the move is intended to “create a vacuum and black out information on very important environmental issues” such as climate change.

Title: Word “Climate” Removed from BIA Program Name
Summary: The “Tribal Climate Resilience Program” was renamed the “Tribal Resilience Program” and all references to “climate” and “climate change” were removed from its website.

Title: Word “Climate” Removed from EPA Program Name and Website
Summary: EPA’s “Climate Ready Water Utilities” program was renamed the “Creating Resilient Water Utilities” program and several references to “climate change” and “climate science” were removed from its website.

Title: Word “Science” Removed from EPA Science Office Website
Summary: References to EPA’s Office of Science and Technology policy developing “science-based standards” were changed to read “economically and technologically achievable standards.”

Title: WSU Researcher Investigated by Washington Legislators
Summary: Washington State Representative Joel Kretz and State Senator Mark Schoesler filed a scientific misconduct complaint against a Washington State University (WSU) professor in relation to a 2014 study he had conducted on wolf predation. Representative Kretz had previously sought to discredit the study, including by having the professor fired by WSU.

Title: National Climate Assessments Removed from NASA Website
Summary: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration has announced that it will not host the Congressionally mandated National Climate Assessments on its website, indicating that the assessments will not be available through any federal website.

Title: Commerce Department Suspends Work on Extreme Rainfall Database
Summary: The Commerce Department has indefinitely suspended work on part of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA)’s Atlas 15 project, which provides critical data for predicting extreme rainfall events and assessing how climate change impacts these events.

Title: Trump Administration Cancels Nearly $500 Million in Funding for mRNA Vaccine Development
Summary: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. announced that the Trump administration is cancelling nearly $500 million in funding earmarked for the development of mRNA vaccines.

Title: NSF Plans to Terminate Lease on U.S. Research Vessel
Summary: The National Science Foundation has proposed terminating its lease on the RV Nathaniel B. Palmer, the only research vessel in the U.S. that can reach Antarctica.

Title: USDA to Close Major Research Site, Forest Research Stations
Summary: The U.S. Department of Agriculture announced the closure of its major research site in Beltsville, Maryland, as well as plans to close most of its forest research stations across the country.

Title: Trump Administration Cuts Funding to NASEM, Forcing Cuts of up to 250 Positions
Summary: The National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine confirmed to staff that up to 250 positions could be cut by the end of the summer after the Trump administration terminated tens of millions of dollars in federal contracts.

Title: NASA Acting Administrator Tells Employees to Plan for Firings, Mission Shutdown
Summary: Sean Duffy, acting administrator of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration, directed employees to plan for significant workforce adjustments and the potential shutdown of various missions, despite warnings that such actions would be illegal prior to a Congressionally approved budget for fiscal year 2026.

Title: DOE Secretary Questions Accuracy of Previous Federal Climate Reports, Says They Will Be Updated
Summary: Department of Energy Secretary Chris Wright said that previously published National Climate Assessments were not “fair” and that they will be “updated.” Experts have expressed concern that the unspecified updates will be used to undermine the scientific consensus on climate change.

Title: Trump Administration Removes EPA Scientific Integrity Policy from Agency Website
Summary: On August 21, 2025, the Trump administration removed the Environmental Protection Agency’s updated scientific integrity policy from the agency’s website.

Title: NSF Suspends Nearly 300 Grants to UCLA
Summary: On July 31, 2025, the National Science Foundation informed the University of California, Los Angeles that it would be indefinitely suspending nearly 300 research grants.

Title: FoodNet–A Coalition of Federal and State Bodies Responsible for Tracking Foodborne Illness–Stops Monitoring Six Pathogens
Summary: As of July 1, 2025, the Foodborne Diseases Active Surveillance Network (FoodNet) program stopped tracking infections caused by six pathogens, reducing surveillance to just two pathogens.

Title: Trump Administration Cuts $1 Billion from Global Vaccine Alliance
Summary: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. announced that the agency will cut over $1 billion in funding previously pledged to Gavi, the international vaccine alliance, citing a 2017 study that some experts have found problematic.

Title: EPA Stops Updating Database Used to Calculate Greenhouse Gas Emissions
Summary: On June 24, 2025, the Environmental Protection Agency announced that it would stop updating the U.S. Environmentally-Extended Input-Output database, which is used by hundreds of companies to calculate greenhouse gas emissions in their supply chains.

Title: HHS Ends Minority Biomedical Research Support Program
Summary: On August 25, 2025, HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr., announced the cancellation of the Minority Biomedical Research Support program, effective September 25, 2025.

Title: Trump Administration Guts HHS’s Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
Summary: On April 1, 2025, the Trump administration gutted the Health and Human Services ’s Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality, slashing its budget by 80 to 90% and firing half of the agency’s staff.

Title: Trump Administration Cuts $15 Million Research into PFAS
Summary: The Trump administration eliminated a $15 million research program focused on understanding how PFAS contaminate U.S. farmland. Research conducted under the program has improved understanding of how pesticides and sewage sludge used to fertilize crops can lead to widespread food contamination.

Title: DOE Staff Given List of Banned Terms
Summary: On September 26, 2025, staff at the Department of Energy's Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy were given a list of words to avoid in both internal and public communications, including “climate change,” “emissions,” “decarbonization,” and “sustainability/sustainable.”

Title: HHS Indefinitely Pauses CDC Infectious Disease Data Project
Summary: In September 2025, the Department of Health and Human Services indefinitely paused a Centers for Disease Control and Prevention infectious disease data project that aimed to provide real-time information on 127 notifiable diseases.

Title: Trump Promotes Debunked Theory Linking Vaccines to Autism
Summary: On September 8, 2025, President Trump posted a video to social media that promoted the widely discredited theory that vaccines cause autism.

Title: Florida Surgeon General Misrepresents Safety of Vaccines
Summary: In announcing plans to end all state vaccine mandates, Florida Surgeon General Joseph Ladapo made several false or misleading claims about the safety of vaccines and described them as “poison.”

Title: DOE Secretary Misrepresents Climate Science
Summary: In announcing the cancellation of an offshore wind farm, Department of Energy Secretary Chris Wright contradicted established climate science, including by suggesting that climate change is not impacting and will not impact America’s day-to-day life.

Title: RFK Jr. Makes False Claims about Covid Deaths, Vaccine Safety at Senate Hearing
Summary: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr., made several false or misleading statements during a Senate Finance Committee hearing, including claiming that “there’s no clinical data” to support Covid vaccine recommendations for healthy people and that “nobody knows” how many Americans have died from Covid.

Title: Trump Administration Cuts $100 Million from NOAA’s Research Arm
Summary: The Trump administration will spend nearly $100 million less on the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration Office of Oceanic and Atmospheric Research than the $745 million approved by Congress for FY2025. The spending cuts primarily affect weather, ocean, and climate research.

Title: CDC Staff Reductions Halt Data Collection on Maternal, Infant Health
Summary: Significant staffing reductions at the CDC have halted data collection for PRAMS, a tool used to track maternal and infant health, prompting Mississippi to suspend data collection during a public health emergency amid rising infant mortality rates.

Title: Information on Climate Change Removed from Acadia National Park
Summary: The National Park Service has taken down several signs at Acadia National Park that explained the effects of climate change on the park, following the removal of climate change information from the NPS website earlier this year.

Title: President Trump Makes Several False Statements about Climate Change at UN
Summary: In a speech to the UN General Assembly, President Trump made several false or misleading statements regarding climate change, including calling it “the greatest con job ever perpetrated on the world.”

Title: EPA Orders Scientists in Office of Water to Halt Publication of New Research
Summary: The Environmental Protection Agency instructed scientists in the Office of Water to halt publication of new research pending implementation of a new review process, which will apply to all publications, including those already completed.

Title: Alaska State Senator Questions Science of Climate Change
Summary: In discussing a bill addressing various energy issues, Senator Shelly Hughes questioned the scientific consensus on climate change and suggested that carbon dioxide emissions do not “truly … impact … the climate.”

Title: EPA Refusing to Release Completed PFAS Toxicity Report
Summary: EPA has not published a report on the toxicity of PFNA, a forever chemical, which was deemed “completed and ready to post” in mid-April.

Title: CDC Journal Not Published During Shutdown
Summary: The Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report is not being published during the federal government shutdown, a change from past shutdowns when publication continued. The last issue was released on September 25.

Title: Secretary of Energy Makes False Statements about Climate Change
Summary: Secretary of Energy Chris Wright made several false or misleading statements about climate change at multiple events on a trip to Europe, including claiming that the climate crisis is “so exaggerated” and that worsening extreme weather is “simply untrue.”

Title: Trump Claims Tylenol Causes Autism in Misrepresentation of Scientific Studies
Summary: President Trump and Secretary of Health and Human Services Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced that acetaminophen may cause autism when taken during pregnancy. Medical and scientific experts have criticized the announcement as inconsistent with current scientific knowledge.

Title: Interior Halts Funding for Climate Adaptation Science Centers
Summary: The Department of the Interior failed to complete the paperwork required to fund the U.S. Geological Survey’s (USGS) Climate Adaptation Science Centers. As a result, the centers are expected to reduce operations and possibly close.

Title: CDC Adds Debunked Link Between Autism and Vaccines to Website
Summary: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) wrongly suggested there is no scientific basis for the view that vaccines do not cause autism when, in fact, numerous studies found no causal link between the two. Scientists have accused the CDC of “promot[ing] false information” about vaccines.

Title: NOAA to Cancel Funding for Data Collection on Tsunami Risk
Summary: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) is cancelling a contract with an organization that records and sends data to NOAA’s National Tsunami Center to warn Alaskan communities of tsunami risks. NOAA has cited a lack of funding as justification for the cancellation.

Title: CDC Removes Webpages on Health Equity and LGBTQ+ Care
Summary: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention removed at least 22 webpages on topics of health equity and health care for LGBTQ+ people, which provided related educational resources.

Title: HHS Publishes Report Pushing Discredited Theory about Gender-Affirming Care
Summary: The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) published a report advancing the disproven theory that gender-affirming care for transgender youth does more harm than good. HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. praised the report, calling gender-affirming care “malpractice” without evidence.

Title: Indian Health Services Shifts Vaccine Messaging, Flags Terms for Additional Review
Summary: Indian Health Services (IHS) officials identified several so-called “buzzwords,” such as “immunizations” and “vaccines,” that require additional approval from IHS public information officers before use in social media posts, pamphlets, and patient presentations.

Title: Vaccine Expert Dismissed from FDA Committee
Summary: Vaccine expert Paul Offit, who had served on the FDA’s Vaccines and Related Biologics Products Advisory Committee since 2017, was removed from the committee.

Title: 4,200 Federal Employees Fired during Government Shutdown
Summary: During the government shutdown, the Trump administration issued reduction-in-force notices to approximately 4,200 federal employees, including 1,300 staff at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention across more than a dozen CDC divisions.

Title: FDA Makes Unjustified Claims about COVID Vaccine Deaths
Summary: The Food and Drug Administration’s Chief Medical and Scientific Officer, Dr. Vinay Prasad, issued a memo claiming that COVID-19 vaccines have killed 10 children in the United States without providing evidence to support that claim, and made other misleading statements about the dangers of COVID-19 and the safety of the vaccine.

Title: EPA Removes References to Human-Caused Climate Change from Its Website
Summary: The Environmental Protection Agency has removed references to the human causes of climate change from its website, along with scientific data that provided evidence of human-caused climate change.

Title: Trump Administration Plans to “Break Up” the U.S.’s Largest Climate Research Center
Summary: On December 16, 2025, Office of Management and Budget Director Russell Vought posted on social media that the Trump administration planned to “break up” the National Center for Atmospheric Research in Colorado, the largest federal climate research center in the U.S.

Title: Trump Administration Shutters Goddard Space Flight Center Library
Summary: On January 2, 2026, the library at the NASA Goddard Space Flight Center closed. The library had housed historical records of past missions, many of which are not digitized or available elsewhere.

Title: Experts Excluded from ACIP Review of Hepatitis B Vaccine
Summary: On December 2, 2025, the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) met to review the hepatitis B vaccine, but vaccine experts from within and outside the federal government reported being shut out of the meeting.

Title: NIH Pauses Grants that Include Terms like “Health Equity” and “Structural Racism”
Summary: The Trump administration has paused funding for NIH grants that include terms such as “health equity” and “structural racism,” and address other topics that do not align with the administration’s priorities, pending further review.

Title: AZ Senator Prefiles Bill Requiring Public Schools to Teach "Intelligent Design"
Summary: An Arizona State Senator prefiled a bill that would require all public schools in the state that teach evolution by natural selection to also teach the theory of “intelligent design.”

Title: Nearly Half of CDC Vaccine Information Databases Not Updated Regularly in 2025
Summary: In 2025, nearly half (38 out of 82) Centers for Disease Control and Prevention databases that are typically updated monthly experienced unexplained pauses, and 34 had not been updated in at least 6 months. 87% of the affected databases reported vaccination information, while none of the databases that continued to be updated regularly were related to vaccines.

Title: Trump Posts False Claims about Climate Change
Summary: On January 23, 2026, President Trump posted false claims about climate change on social media, asking that “Environmental Insurrectionists please explain — WHATEVER HAPPENED TO GLOBAL WARMING???”

Title: Trump Admin Ends Financial Support for NASA Advisory Groups
Summary: On January 16, 2026, the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) announced that it would end financial support for 8 independent planetary science advisory groups.

Title: RFK Jr. Makes False Claims about Fluoride, Vaccines, and Infectious Diseases
Summary: In an interview on January 6, 2026, Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr., made several false or misleading claims about vaccines, fluoride, 5G cell towers, and infectious diseases.

Title: RFK Jr Touts Keto Diet as ‘Cure’ for Mental Illness
Summary: At a public event, Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr., made false and misleading claims about mental health and eating habits, suggesting that a ketogenic diet could “cure” schizophrenia.

Title: Intelligent Design Bill Introduced in Oklahoma State Senate
Summary: A bill has been introduced in the Oklahoma State Senate that, if enacted, would require public and charter school teachers who teach evolution to also "provide instruction to students on the concepts of creationism and/or intelligent design."

Title: NIH Ends Funding for Research Using Human Fetal Tissue
Summary: On January 22, 2026, President Trump announced that the National Institutes of Health would no longer fund biomedical research that uses human fetal tissue.

Title: DOE Lays Off 134 Employees at Renewable Energy Lab
Summary: The Department of Energy laid off 134 employees, including scientists and operations staff, at the National Laboratory of the Rockies (formerly the National Renewable Energy Laboratory).

Title: President Trump Calls Climate Change’s Threats to Public Health a “Scam”
Summary: Following the Environmental Protection Agency’s repeal of the 2009 Endangerment Finding, President Trump stated that the science establishing that climate change negatively impacts public health is “a scam.”

Title: Alabama Governor Signs Law Restricting Use of Scientific Research in Policy-Making
Summary: On February 19, 2026, Alabama Governor Kay Ivey signed a bill into law that restricts the types of scientific research that can be used to set environmental regulations. Critics argue that the restrictions create an “insurmountable burden of proof for agency rulemakings that is out of step with scientific practice.”

Title: Anti-Vaccine Theorist Appointed to CDC Advisory Committee Reviewing Covid-19 Vaccines
Summary: A review of Covid-19 vaccine safety is being led by a known vaccine skeptic, who lacks relevant qualifications. His prior work on vaccine safety has been criticized by experts for failing to meet basic scientific standards.
